nodes.title: The data: 6 redshift bins across the RR2 footprint
nodes.body:
The redshift dimension. For what the fields look like on the sky, see `tapestry:plot_field_overview`. This node shows how those galaxies are distributed in redshift ŌĆö the tomographic binning that gives the cross-correlation its depth information.

**Six bins from z Ōēł 0.2 to 2.0.** The Euclid RR2 v2.1 weak lensing catalog contains ~50 million galaxies with shape measurements from two independent methods (lensmc and metacal). Tomographic bins are defined by the photometric pipeline, with n(z) distributions from `RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits`. The effective sky fraction is ~1% per bin at nside 2048.

**CMB side.** ACT DR6 (ILC reconstruction) and SPT-3G winter (GMV + TT/PP/MV variants). ACT overlaps the full Euclid footprint; SPT covers only the southern patch (Dec < -42┬░).

**28 baseline spectra.** C_Ōäō^{╬║E} in 20 log-spaced bandpowers (Ōäō = 40ŌĆō3000): 2 methods ├Ś 7 bins ├Ś 2 CMB experiments. These are the inputs to everything downstream ŌĆö the signal overview (`tapestry:plot_signal_overview`), consistency tests, null tests, and theory comparison.

**Covariance.** Knox formula throughout: Žā┬▓(C_b) = C^{╬║╬║}_b ┬Ę C^{╬│╬│}_b / n_modes, where n_modes = ╬Ż(2Ōäō+1) ┬Ę f_sky^eff. The effective sky fraction f_sky^eff = Ō¤©wŌéüwŌééŌ¤®┬▓ / Ō¤©wŌéü┬▓wŌéé┬▓Ō¤® (Hivon 2002) accounts for the apodized weight maps. Auto-spectra C^{╬║╬║} and C^{╬│╬│} are computed from the masked maps (not catalog-based). This is the diagonal of the covariance ŌĆö no off-diagonal (bin-bin) correlations. Knox is preferred over NaMaster Gaussian, which overestimates variance by 10-130├Ś at our fsky.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/compare_nz_distributions.py`

## Comments
**2026-02-15 23:44** ŌĆö n(z) plot needs updating: (1) Plot the actual shear n(z) from the 6-bin file (RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits), not the forecasted version. May need smoothing. (2) Empirical check: take bin IDs from the catalog and plot by photo-z median ŌĆö verify bins match the n(z) file. (3) Galaxy clustering n(z) is optional for now, stick with shear.
nodes.outcome: Euclid RR2 v2.1 weak lensing catalog: ~50M galaxies, 6 tomographic bins, nside 2048. n(z) from theoretical pipeline (RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits). Effective sky fraction ~0.01 per bin.

nodes.title: ACT vs SPT: same galaxies, same spectrum
nodes.body:
The same Euclid shear field crossed with two independent CMB lensing reconstructions ŌĆö ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV ŌĆö should give the same spectrum. If it doesn't, something is wrong on the CMB side.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (lensmc baseline) shows it directly: salmon ACT and teal SPT data points track each other across all bins and multipoles. You can see both the agreement and the noise structure ŌĆö SPT error bars widen at low Ōäō where its smaller sky area limits the mode count, while ACT's larger footprint gives tighter constraints there. At high Ōäō they converge. All 6 bins pass the shape consistency test (median chi2/dof = 0.86, median PTE = 0.63, 0 failures at 5%).

The agreement holds despite ACT and SPT showing different *amplitudes* relative to theory (A_lens = 0.93 vs 0.76). The chi2 test compares shape, not normalization ŌĆö it cannot detect a uniform multiplicative offset. The SPT deficit is exactly that: a uniform ~24% suppression, consistent with a CMB-side calibration factor rather than Ōäō-dependent systematics.

A subtlety beneath the agreement: ACT is contaminated by stellar density and galactic extinction (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`: bias significance up to 8Žā in some bins), while SPT is clean (<0.1Žā after marginalization). This doesn't violate the consistency test because the contamination has the same sign as the signal ŌĆö it inflates ACT, shifting its normalization up, but the shape remains consistent with SPT.

For the method-independence test (lensmc vs metacal), see `tapestry:plot_method_comparison`. For SPT estimator variants, see `tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`.

## Comments
**2026-02-16 00:39** ŌĆö ACT vs SPT comparison plot needs proper xlims ŌĆö current axis range doesn't show the data well.
nodes.outcome: ACT and SPT cross-spectra are fully consistent: 14/14 pass at 5% level, median PTE=0.639, median chi2/dof=0.86. No evidence of CMB-side systematics. Evidence + plots co-located at results/claims/plot_act_vs_spt/.

nodes.title: Redshift trend: signal rises with lensing kernel overlap
nodes.body:
All 6 tomographic bins on a single panel, testing the basic lensing prediction: the cross-correlation amplitude should increase with source redshift, because the CMB ╬║ lensing kernel (peaking at z ~ 2) has more overlap with higher-redshift galaxies.

**It does.** S/N ranges from ~5Žā (bin 1ŌĆō2, low lensing kernel overlap) to ~12Žā (bin 6, maximum overlap). The ordering matches ╬øCDM predictions. Theory overlays (Planck 2018 with standard NLA IA) track the measured redshift dependence.

**Low-z complication.** Bins 1ŌĆō2 are where two effects compete. Systematic contamination from stellar density *boosts* the ACT signal (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`), while intrinsic alignments *suppress* the theory prediction (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). In raw data, these partially cancel ŌĆö the fit looks good, but for the wrong reasons. After template marginalization (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`), the low-z ACT amplitude drops. The redshift trend with raw data is diagnostic, not definitive, for the low bins.

**High-z clean.** Bins 4ŌĆō6 are clean on both sides: contamination is negligible, IA suppression is small (<10%), and the ACT data matches theory well. These bins carry the bulk of the cosmological constraining power.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_redshift_trend.py`
nodes.outcome: Signal increases with redshift bin as expected from lensing kernel overlap. S/N 5-12, highest in bin 6. Low-z bins (1-2) complicated by contamination + IA degeneracy.

nodes.title: lensmc vs metacal: pipeline independence
nodes.body:
Two independent shear measurement pipelines ŌĆö lensmc (forward-model PSF fitting) and metacal (metacalibration with sheared images) ŌĆö process the same galaxies in different ways. If their cross-spectra with CMB ╬║ agree, neither introduces a measurement-specific bias.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (ACT baseline) makes the agreement visible: blue lensmc and orange metacal data points sit on top of each other in every bin, with fractional differences scattering around zero in the sub-panels. The residuals are mostly within ┬▒10% even at noisy bandpowers. Median chi2/dof = 0.14 ŌĆö suspiciously tight, but expected. The two methods share the *same galaxies* and the *same CMB map*, so their noise is highly correlated. The Knox formula assumes independent noise realizations; applied to the difference of correlated measurements it dramatically overestimates the variance, deflating chi2. This test confirms consistency but has limited discriminating power. A proper test would need a covariance for the difference spectrum accounting for shared noise.

Despite producing consistent cross-spectra, the two methods couple differently to systematic contamination. After template marginalization, lensmc retains 75% of its raw S/N (20Žā ŌåÆ 15Žā) while metacal retains 69% (19Žā ŌåÆ 13Žā). The different shear weight maps couple differently to spatially-varying systematics. Both methods give consistent final results, but lensmc is preferable for contamination-sensitive analyses.
nodes.outcome: 35 comparisons (7 bins x 5 CMB experiments) all pass. Median chi2/dof=0.14, PTE=1.00 ŌĆö low chi2 expected from correlated noise (same galaxies, same CMB map). No evidence of shear measurement method bias. metacal ~2x more sensitive to systematic contamination than lensmc (different weight coupling).

nodes.title: Theory comparison: Planck 2018 predicts the ACT signal
nodes.body:
The hero figure (`tapestry:plot_signal_overview`) shows data against vanilla Planck ╬øCDM. Here we add the full theory: ╬øCDM + NLA intrinsic alignment (A_IA=1.72, ╬Ę=-0.41 from Euclid pipeline defaults ŌĆö see `nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). Both curves are predictions, not fits: cosmology from Planck, IA from Euclid, binned through NaMaster bandpower windows.

The plot shows two theory lines per bin: vanilla Planck 2018 (black) and NLA IA (crimson dashed). The IA correction suppresses the prediction by 4-36% depending on redshift ŌĆö largest at low-z where the GI term geometry is most favorable.

**ACT is consistent with standard theory.** Joint chi2 = 106/120 (PTE 0.82) against the NLA prediction. The vanilla prediction slightly overshoots ŌĆö adding standard IA brings it into line. This isn't a fit; it's a prediction matching data.

**SPT shows a 24% deficit.** Joint chi2 = 226/120 (PTE Ōēł 0). Data sits below both theory curves. The deficit is method-independent (lensmc Ōēł metacal), scale-dependent (worse at high Ōäō ŌĆö see `tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`), and absent from ACT. This points to a CMB-side normalization issue, characterized further in `tapestry:plot_theory_residual_analysis`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_theory_comparison.py`
nodes.outcome: ACT consistent with Planck 2018 ╬øCDM + standard NLA IA (PTE 0.82). SPT shows 24% normalization deficit (PTEŌēł0), method-independent, CMB-side. Shown: lensmc ├Ś {ACT, SPT GMV} ŌĆö metacal and SPT variants covered by method_comparison and spt_estimator nodes.

nodes.title: Contamination metric: small per bandpower, dangerous in aggregate
nodes.body:
A systematic can only contaminate a cross-correlation if it correlates with *both* tracers simultaneously. The contamination metric X^f_S(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S} ┬Ę C^{fS} / C^{SS}, following DES├ŚSPT (2203.12440 ┬¦A.4), quantifies exactly this: it has the same units as the signal C^{f╬║} and can be compared directly to the measurement uncertainty.

The composite figure shows X(Ōäō) for all five systematics (stellar density, galactic extinction, exposures, zodiacal light, SAA) crossed with the two baseline CMB reconstructions (ACT and SPT-GMV). Six redshift bins are overlaid in each panel; gray bands mark 1% and 10% of the signal Knox uncertainty. Per bandpower, all contamination metrics sit within or below the 1% band ŌĆö small enough to look safe. Even galactic extinction, which individually contaminates both ╬║ (chi2 ~ 49ŌĆō69/20) and shear (chi2/dof up to 9 in the null tests), produces sub-percent per-bandpower bias. This is the fundamental advantage of cross-correlations: contamination requires a triple coincidence.

But per-bandpower smallness is misleading. Twenty bandpowers each contributing <1% can integrate to a multi-Žā bias. The C^{SS} denominator carries noise bias from downgrading sparse systematic maps (nside 16384ŌåÆ2048), inflating the denominator and making X a *lower bound*. The bias budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) integrates over multipoles and reveals that stellar density reaches 4.4Žā and galactic extinction 3.7Žā for ACT ŌĆö individually small, collectively dangerous. Where the budget flags significant bias, template marginalization handles it (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`).
nodes.outcome: Per-bandpower contamination metric X = C^{╬║S}┬ĘC^{fS}/C^{SS} sits below 1% of signal uncertainty for all five systematics across all CMB estimators. However, the integrated bias significance (tapestry:plot_systematic_budget) reaches multi-Žā levels for stellar density and galactic extinction ŌĆö per-bandpower smallness does not guarantee negligible integrated bias.

nodes.title: Systematic null tests: galactic extinction correlates with shear
nodes.body:
Do observational systematics correlate with the shear signal? This is the first-pass diagnostic ŌĆö cross-correlate each systematic map with the Euclid shear field and test whether the result is consistent with zero.

The 2├Ś3 panel shows per-bandpower pulls (C^{fS}_Ōäō / Žā_Knox) for all five systematics across six tomographic bins. Under the null hypothesis, pulls scatter around zero with unit variance ŌĆö the ┬▒1Žā and ┬▒2Žā gray bands mark the expected range. Coherent excursions beyond ┬▒2Žā signal contamination; their multipole structure shows *where* in harmonic space the systematic leaks in.

**Galactic extinction stands out.** In bins 5 and 6 (highest redshift), the pink pulls reach ┬▒3-5Žā across a broad range of multipoles ŌĆö this is broadband contamination, not a feature at a specific angular scale. The chi2/dof climbs to 7.9 in bin 6. Lower bins show milder excursions (chi2/dof 1.5-2.3). The physical mechanism: Galactic dust affects PSF modeling and photometric calibration, imprinting a large-scale pattern on the shear maps.

**Other systematics are clean.** Stellar density, exposures, SAA, and zodiacal light all scatter within the ┬▒1Žā band in most bins. Stellar density shows occasional 2Žā excursions but no coherent spectral pattern ŌĆö its null test looks benign. This is misleading: the bias budget analysis (`plot_systematic_budget`) reveals stellar density is actually the *dominant* ACT contaminant, because its correlation with CMB ╬║ is stronger than galactic extinction's. The null test only measures C^{fS} (shear ├Ś systematic); the full contamination diagnostic requires C^{╬║S} as well.

**What this test cannot see.** A systematic that correlates with CMB ╬║ but not with shear won't appear here. The contamination metric (`tapestry:plot_contamination_metric`) captures the joint effect ŌĆö and reveals that even galactic extinction's alarming null test failures translate to sub-percent per-bandpower bias, because the triple coincidence required for cross-correlation contamination is much rarer than pairwise correlations suggest. But sub-percent per bandpower can integrate to multi-Žā bias over 20 bandpowers ŌĆö the integrated budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) and template marginalization (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`) complete the picture.
nodes.outcome: Spectral pull plot: 5 systematics ├Ś 6 bins showing C^{fS}/Žā_Knox per bandpower. Galactic extinction shows coherent ┬▒3-5Žā pulls at low Ōäō in bins 5-6 (chi2/dof up to 7.9). Clean systematics scatter within ┬▒1Žā. 7/30 lensmc tests fail at 5%. The spectral structure is now visible ŌĆö contamination is broadband, not confined to specific multipoles.

nodes.title: Catalog validation: pixelization introduces no bias
nodes.body:
NmtFieldCatalog works directly from galaxy catalogs, bypassing pixelization. NmtField works from maps built by binning galaxies into HEALPix pixels. If the two give the same cross-spectrum, map construction introduces no bias. Same galaxies, same CMB ╬║, different computational paths.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (lensmc ├Ś ACT) shows them side by side: blue map-based with error bars, orange catalog-based without. They track each other across all bins and multipoles ŌĆö Pearson correlation 0.995-0.999, with the closest agreement at high Ōäō where signal dominates. The RMS fractional differences (7-33%, S/N-weighted) reflect noise scatter in low-S/N bandpowers, not systematic bias. No trend with redshift bin.

This validates the map-based pipeline as the production measurement. The catalog approach serves as an independent check, not a competing estimator ŌĆö it's computationally heavier (64GB for ~5M galaxies at lmax=3000) and provides no covariance estimate.
nodes.outcome: Map-based and catalog-based cross-spectra agree: median correlation 0.998, RMS 7-33% (S/N-weighted). Map pipeline validated ŌĆö no pixelization bias.

nodes.title: SPT estimators: no foreground bias
nodes.body:
SPT-3G provides four CMB lensing estimator variants with genuinely different foreground sensitivity. GMV (baseline) uses all quadratic pairs ŌĆö highest SNR but most foreground-exposed. TT (temperature-only) is maximally sensitive to tSZ from galaxy clusters. PP (polarization-only) is the cleanest but noisiest. MV (SQE) tests pipeline-level consistency. All share the same survey mask, so differences in their cross-spectra with Euclid shear isolate foreground contamination in the lensing reconstruction.

The 6-panel ratio plot (lensmc baseline) shows each variant divided by GMV as a function of multipole. The ratios scatter around unity in every bin ŌĆö TT (salmon), PP (teal), MV (purple) all consistent with the 10% shaded band, with noise increasing at low Ōäō where each variant's individual S/N is lowest. PP shows the widest scatter (noisiest estimator), but no systematic offset. TT ŌĆö the critical test for tSZ bias ŌĆö tracks GMV tightly. The large error bars at individual bandpowers correctly show that this is a noisy test: the power comes from the aggregate (18 comparisons, median chi2/dof = 0.19), not any single Ōäō bin.

This means tSZ contamination of the SPT lensing reconstruction does not bias the shear ├Ś ╬║ cross-correlation at current noise levels. The SPT normalization deficit (A_lens = 0.76) cannot be explained by foreground leakage ŌĆö it persists identically across all four estimators, including the foreground-immune PP channel. This disfavors foreground-related hypotheses for the deficit (see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`).

## Comments
**2026-02-15 23:56** ŌĆö Plot rework: drop the ratio plot (noise/noise ratios scatter too much at low Ōäō). Instead plot differences relative to best-fit Planck amplitude: (C_variant - C_GMV) / C_theory(A_lens). Clean residual in interpretable units.
nodes.outcome: All 14 comparisons (2 methods x 7 bins) show complete consistency across all 4 SPT estimator variants. Chi2/dof: MV~0.03, TT~0.2, PP~0.3 (all vs GMV, dof=20). Min PTE=0.981 (metacal bin3 PP). No foreground contamination detected ŌĆö tSZ bias in TT estimator does not affect shear x kappa cross-spectra at current noise levels. 42 cross-spectra computed, 14 comparison plots + evidence at results/claims/plot_spt_estimator_comparison/.

nodes.title: Jackknife: galaxy-side systematics are clean
nodes.body:
Multiply each galaxy's ellipticity by a random sign (┬▒1), destroying the coherent cosmological signal while preserving noise structure, pixel coverage, and weight maps. Cross-correlating these randomized shear maps with CMB ╬║ should yield spectra consistent with zero. Any excess reveals galaxy-side additive systematics ŌĆö PSF leakage, selection effects, calibration errors correlated with sky position.

All 24 null spectra (2 methods ├Ś 6 bins ├Ś 2 CMB experiments, 20 bandpowers each) are consistent with zero: median chi2/dof = 0.88, median PTE = 0.62, zero failures at 5%. The galaxy side is clean.

This test is sensitive to additive systematics but blind to multiplicative biases (shear calibration), which are sign-invariant. A single random realization (seed=42) suffices to rule out large additive contamination. For the CMB-side null test (uncorrelated simulations), see `tapestry:plot_sim_null_test`. For spatial structure, see `tapestry:plot_spatial_variation`.
nodes.outcome: 24/24 null cross-spectra (2 methods x 6 bins x 2 CMB) consistent with zero. Median Knox chi2/dof=0.88, median PTE=0.617, 0 failures at 5% threshold. No galaxy-side systematics detected.

nodes.title: Theory residuals: ACT passes, SPT fails in shape
nodes.body:
The theory comparison (`tapestry:plot_theory_comparison`) shows ACT matching and SPT low. But how much of the SPT deficit is a simple amplitude offset vs. a shape disagreement? This node fits a single lensing amplitude A_lens per bin and tests whether the residual shape ŌĆö data/(├é┬Ętheory) ŌĆö is consistent with noise.

Theory here is NLA IA (see `nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`), the same prediction shown in the theory comparison. Amplitude fits use Knox covariance (diagonal).

| CMB | A_lens | Žā from 1 | shape PTE |
|-----|-------|----------|-----------|
| ACT lensmc | 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 | 1.3Žā | 0.83 |
| SPT-GMV lensmc | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 | 4.9Žā | ~0 |

**ACT passes both tests.** A_lens = 0.93 ŌĆö within 1.3Žā of unity. Shape is excellent. The ~7% residual is consistent with known missing model ingredients (`theory-model-completeness-for-526d66a5`): shear calibration ~2%, baryonic feedback ~3-5%, n(z) shifts ~1-3%.

**SPT fails in both amplitude and shape.** The deficit has bin-level structure: low-z bins (1-3) fail the shape test (chi2/dof 1.7-2.7) while high-z bins (4-6) pass (chi2/dof 1.2-1.6). The Ōäō-range robustness test (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`) reveals scale dependence: high-Ōäō bandpowers actively suppress the amplitude, disfavoring a uniform calibration offset. This is characterized further in `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.
nodes.outcome: Per-bin amplitude fits: ACT A_lens=0.93┬▒0.06 (1.3Žā from unity, shape PTE 0.83), SPT A_lens=0.76┬▒0.05 (4.9Žā, shape fails). ACT consistent with ╬øCDM; SPT deficit is scale-dependent and method-independent.

nodes.title: Analysis summary: one-page synthesis
nodes.body:
Everything in one figure. Four panels compress the full analysis ŌĆö detection, consistency, null tests, theory ŌĆö into a single page, reading aggregate evidence from all upstream nodes. For the spectra themselves, start with `tapestry:plot_signal_overview`.

## (a) Detection

Knox S/N per tomographic bin. All 12 baseline spectra detected at >3Žā (11/12 at >5Žā). S/N rises with bin number ŌĆö more lensing kernel overlap at higher source redshift. Two headline numbers: **20Žā (ACT) and 21Žā (SPT) raw**, **15Žā (ACT) and 14Žā (SPT) after systematic marginalization**. The marginalized number accounts for contamination via Fisher matrix template fitting (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`).

## (b) Consistency

Four cross-checks, all passing. ACT vs SPT (`tapestry:plot_act_vs_spt`: 14/14 at 5%, PTE 0.64). Lensmc vs metacal (`tapestry:plot_method_comparison`: 35/35, PTE 1.0). SPT estimator variants (`tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`: 14/14, PTE 1.0). Catalog vs map (`tapestry:plot_catalog_validation_summary`: 28/28, correlation 0.998).

## (c) Null tests

Jackknife (`tapestry:plot_jackknife_null_test`: 0/24 fail, chi2/dof 0.88). Simulation null (`tapestry:plot_sim_null_test`: 1/66 false alarm, chi2/dof 0.92). Spatial split (`tapestry:plot_spatial_variation`: 0/6 fail at Ōäō>100). Ōäō-range robustness (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`: ACT stable, SPT scale-dependent). Systematic null tests (`tapestry:plot_systematic_null_tests`: galactic extinction fails 10/14, others clean).

## (d) ╬øCDM consistency

Amplitudes against Planck 2018 ╬øCDM + NLA IA (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). **Before contamination handling** (`tapestry:plot_theory_residual_analysis`): ACT A_lens=0.93┬▒0.06 (1.3Žā from unity), SPT A_lens=0.76┬▒0.05 (4.9Žā). **After template marginalization** (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`): ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06, chi2/dof=0.75 (PTE 0.98) ŌĆö consistent. SPT A_lens=0.75, chi2/dof=1.61 ŌĆö deficit persists, see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_analysis_summary.py`
nodes.outcome: One-page 4-panel summary. (a) Detection: ACT 20Žā raw / 15Žā marginalized, SPT 21Žā / 14Žā. (b) All 4 consistency tests pass. (c) Null tests: jackknife, sim, spatial all clean; galactic extinction contaminates. (d) ╬øCDM consistency: ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06 after marginalization (PTE 0.98); SPT deficit persists (chi2/dof=1.6).

nodes.title: Fiducial likelihood: 20Žā detection, ╬øCDM consistency
nodes.body:
The headline numbers. Joint 6-bin chi2 evaluation of the full data vector against Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory (with standard NLA IA), using block-diagonal Knox covariance.

## Detection

| Combination | S/N (raw) | S/N (marginalized) |
|-------------|-----------|---------------------|
| lensmc ├Ś ACT | 20Žā | 15Žā |
| metacal ├Ś ACT | 19Žā | ŌĆö |
| lensmc ├Ś SPT | 21Žā | 14Žā |
| metacal ├Ś SPT | 20Žā | ŌĆö |

Raw S/N is data vs zero. Marginalized S/N accounts for systematic contamination via Fisher matrix template marginalization (stellar density, galactic extinction, and other survey systematics jointly fit alongside the signal amplitude).

## ╬øCDM consistency

| Combination | chi2/dof | PTE | A_lens |
|-------------|----------|-----|-------|
| lensmc ├Ś ACT | 0.88 | 0.82 | 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 |
| metacal ├Ś ACT | 0.88 | 0.81 | 0.91 ┬▒ 0.06 |
| lensmc ├Ś SPT | 1.88 | Ōēł0 | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 |
| metacal ├Ś SPT | 1.84 | Ōēł0 | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 |

**ACT ├Ś Euclid is consistent with ╬øCDM.** Chi2/dof = 0.88 (PTE 0.82). A prediction from Planck CMB-only cosmology matches Euclid weak lensing ├Ś ACT CMB lensing at the few-percent level. The amplitude A_lens = 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 is 1.3Žā from unity.

**SPT detects the signal but fails the fit.** 21Žā detection, but chi2/dof = 1.88 (PTE Ōēł 0). The deficit (A_lens = 0.76) is method-independent and persists in shape ŌĆö not a simple calibration offset. This is a CMB-side normalization issue, open for investigation.

## Caveats

Block-diagonal Knox covariance (no cross-bin terms). No CMB ╬║ transfer function correction. Theory at Planck 2018 best-fit with NLA IA at Euclid pipeline defaults (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`) ŌĆö a fixed prediction, not a fit. For the post-marginalization likelihood, see `tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`. For missing theory ingredients, see `theory-model-completeness-for-526d66a5`.
nodes.outcome: Detection: ACT 20Žā, SPT 21Žā. ╬øCDM consistency: ACT chi2/dof=0.88 (PTE 0.82); SPT chi2/dof=1.88 (PTEŌēł0). After systematic marginalization: ACT 15Žā, SPT 14Žā.

nodes.title: Simulation null: CMB-side systematics are clean
nodes.body:
The complement to the jackknife: instead of randomizing the galaxy side, swap the CMB ╬║ map for simulated realizations. SPT provides 11 sims (1 Gaussian + 10 Agora with realistic foregrounds) processed through the same `healqest` lensing pipeline. None share the real convergence field, so cross-correlation with Euclid shear should be zero.

66 null cross-spectra (11 sims ├Ś 6 bins, lensmc ├Ś SPT GMV) are consistent with zero: median chi2/dof = 0.92, median PTE = 0.56, 1 false alarm at 5% (expected ~3.3). No spurious correlations. No mean-field bias. The Agora sims ŌĆö which include tSZ, CIB, and radio sources ŌĆö show no elevated signal compared to the Gaussian sim, ruling out foreground contamination of the SPT lensing reconstruction at our noise level.

The empirical bandpower scatter across sims also calibrates our error bars. The Knox-to-empirical variance ratio is 0.83 ŌĆö Knox overestimates by ~17%, making it slightly conservative. This is expected: Knox uses total auto-spectra (signal + noise) in the numerator. Together with the galaxy-side jackknife (`tapestry:plot_jackknife_null_test`), both sides of the cross-correlation are validated independently.
nodes.outcome: 66 null cross-spectra (11 SPT sims x 6 bins, lensmc x SPT GMV). Median chi2/dof=0.92, median PTE=0.56, 1/66 false alarm at 5% (expected ~3.3). Knox covariance validated: empirical variance ratio 0.83 (Knox slightly conservative). No spurious correlations.

nodes.title: Spatial variation: north and south agree
nodes.body:
Split the RR2 footprint into north and south patches and compute independent cross-spectra in each. This tests for spatial systematics ŌĆö the one dimension the jackknife (which randomizes galaxy signs uniformly across the sky) cannot probe.

North and south patches give consistent cross-spectra at Ōäō > 100: 6/6 bins pass (median chi2/dof = 1.34, PTE = 0.16). At the full Ōäō range, 5/6 bins fail ŌĆö but this is an artifact of the tiny patch sky fractions (fsky ~ 0.001-0.003), where the lowest bandpowers contain less than one mode and Knox variance is unreliable. ACT-only: the SPT winter field (Dec < -42┬░) doesn't overlap the north patch.

If the SPT normalization deficit were spatial ŌĆö stronger in one region ŌĆö the two patches would show different amplitudes. Both patches show the same deficit, pointing to a global calibration issue rather than a spatially varying systematic. This rules out spatially-dependent shear calibration or mask-boundary effects. For the full SPT deficit characterization, see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.
nodes.outcome: 6/6 bins PASS at ell>100 (median chi2/dof=1.34, PTE=0.16, 0 failures). Full-range inflated (5/6 fail) due to <1 mode per low-ell bandpower at patch fsky~0.001. ACT-only (SPT lacks north coverage). No spatial systematics detected.

nodes.title: The signal: CMB lensing ├Ś cosmic shear in six redshift bins
nodes.body:
Six panels ŌĆö one per tomographic redshift bin ŌĆö showing measured C_Ōäō^{╬║E} for both ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV, overlaid with vanilla Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory. This is the cross-correlation: the first thing to look at.

**Detection in every bin.** S/N ranges from 5Žā (bin 2, smallest lensing kernel overlap) to 12Žā (bin 6, maximum overlap). All 24 method ├Ś bin ├Ś CMB combinations detected at >3Žā. Combined: 20Žā (ACT), 21Žā (SPT). The cross-correlation is unambiguous.

**ACT traces Planck ╬øCDM.** Data follows theory in both shape and amplitude ŌĆö a prediction from CMB-only cosmology matching Euclid weak lensing at the percent level, no free parameters. For the detailed theory comparison including intrinsic alignment corrections, see `tapestry:plot_theory_comparison` and the NLA model documentation (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`).

**SPT sits low.** Same spectral shape, ~24% below theory. The offset is coherent across bins and methods ŌĆö a CMB-side normalization issue, not a cosmological discrepancy. The Ōäō-range robustness analysis (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`) and SPT estimator comparisons (`tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`) help characterize this.

**Error bars** are Knox formula ŌĆö diagonal covariance from analytic mode counting (`tapestry:compare_nz_distributions` documents the methodology). ACT: salmon circles. SPT: teal squares. Planck 2018 ╬øCDM: black line (vanilla, no IA). Ōäō┬ĘC_Ōäō scaling. Lensmc representative; metacal in `tapestry:plot_method_comparison`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_signal_overview.py`
nodes.outcome: 6-panel grid showing ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV cross-spectra with vanilla Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory (no IA). Detection in all bins (S/N 5-12). ACT consistent with theory; SPT systematically low. NLA IA in separate fiber.

nodes.title: Bias budget: stellar density is the dominant ACT contaminant
nodes.body:
Quantitative summary of systematic contamination using the DES├ŚSPT bias significance metric. The contamination metric X(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S}_Ōäō ┬Ę C^{fS}_Ōäō / C^{SS}_Ōäō has the same units as the signal C^{E╬║}_Ōäō. The bias significance S_bias = sqrt(╬Ż_Ōäō X┬▓/Žā┬▓) measures how many Žā of bias each systematic contributes, integrating over all Ōäō-bins.

**Key finding**: stellar density is the dominant systematic for ACT (4.4Žā in bin 5, worse than galactic extinction at 3.7Žā). This wasn't apparent from the C^{╬│S} null tests alone, which flagged galactic extinction as worse ŌĆö the bias significance weights by the systematic's correlation with *both* tracers simultaneously (C^{╬║S}/C^{SS}), and stellar density has stronger proportional correlation with CMB ╬║.

**ACT vs SPT asymmetry**: ACT's larger sky coverage admits more galactic contamination. SPT's deep winter field is almost entirely clean (<1Žā across all systematics and bins). The asymmetry is geographic, not instrumental.

**Implications**: S_bias ~ 4.4 against a signal S/N ~ 20 implies ~20% fractional bias in the worst bin ŌĆö significant for precision cosmology. Template marginalization handles this (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`); the deprojection (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`) shows the per-bin impact.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_systematic_budget.py`
nodes.outcome: Heatmap of bias significance S_bias = sqrt(╬Ż_Ōäō X(Ōäō)┬▓/Žā(Ōäō)┬▓) across 5 systematics ├Ś 6 bins ├Ś 2 CMB baselines. ACT: stellar density worst (max 4.4Žā bin 5, all 6 bins >1Žā), galactic extinction second (max 3.7Žā bin 5), exposures+SAA moderate (~1-1.6Žā). SPT: almost all <1Žā (only galactic extinction bin 1 reaches 1.0Žā). Zodiacal light has no auto-power (C^{SS}Ōēł0), contamination undefined but negligible. ACT/SPT asymmetry from larger ACT sky coverage admitting more galactic contamination. 22/48 entries above 1Žā for lensmc, 3 above 3Žā. Method-independent (metacal similar pattern).

nodes.title: Template deprojection: contamination boosts ACT signal
nodes.body:
The contamination metric (`tapestry:plot_contamination_metric`) tells us *how much* each systematic contaminates the signal. The bias budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) maps out where. Deprojection asks: what happens when we account for it?

The answer is surprising. The total contamination X_total (summed over all five systematics) is predominantly positive for ACT ŌĆö it *boosts* the measured cross-spectrum rather than corrupting it with noise. Subtracting it reduces the amplitude. Bin 1 is devastated: after naive subtraction, only 26% of the expected signal remains. Stellar density carried roughly 70% of what was measured in that bin. Bin 5 drops by 25%. The weighted mean amplitude shifts from A_lens = 0.93 to 0.80.

SPT is untouched: the amplitude shifts by less than 0.1Žā after deprojection. This cleanly confirms the SPT normalization deficit is intrinsic ŌĆö it has nothing to do with systematic contamination from the galaxy side.

**Naive subtraction is superseded.** The per-bin bar chart shows naive template subtraction, which *injects template noise* into the chi2 (the X^T C^{-1} X term dominates when templates are noisy). The authoritative values come from Fisher matrix template marginalization: d = A┬Ętheory + ╬Ż ╬▒_S┬ĘX_S, fit jointly. ACT lensmc A_lens = 0.86┬▒0.06, profile chi2/dof = 0.75, PTE = 0.98. The naive per-bin view is diagnostic ŌĆö it shows *where* contamination acts. The marginalized annotation on the plot is the quotable number.

The C^{SS} denominator carries noise bias from sparse systematic maps, making X a lower bound on true contamination. Despite this, the contamination has a clear positive sign. ACT parameter inference should marginalize over contamination templates, or restrict to bins 2ŌĆō4, 6.
nodes.outcome: Contamination BOOSTS ACT signal ŌĆö positive bias from stellar density and galactic extinction. Fisher template marginalization: ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06 (chi2/dof=0.75, PTE 0.98). SPT unaffected. Shown: lensmc ├Ś ACT spectral overlay + amplitude shift bar chart with marginalized annotation.

metrics.caveat: Naive subtraction shown for diagnostics; marginalized (Fisher) values are authoritative.

nodes.title: Template marginalization: ACT consistent after joint fit
nodes.body:
Jointly fit the signal amplitude alongside contamination templates: d = A┬Ętheory + ╬Ż ╬▒_S┬ĘX_S. The Fisher matrix marginalizes over template amplitudes, properly propagating their noise into the uncertainty on A_lens.

**ACT is consistent with ╬øCDM after marginalization.** A_lens = 0.86┬▒0.06 (lensmc), 1.2Žā from unity. Profile chi2/dof = 0.75, PTE = 0.98. Detection significance: 15Žā. The raw A_lens = 0.93 was partly contamination-inflated (stellar density and galactic extinction boost the signal ŌĆö `tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`), but the underlying measurement agrees with Planck 2018 + NLA IA. ACT metacal: A_lens = 0.82┬▒0.06, chi2/dof = 0.82, 13Žā.

**SPT is unaffected.** Chi2/dof = 1.6 persists, marginalized A_lens = 0.75. Contamination is negligible for SPT (<0.1Žā shift). The deficit is CMB-side (`spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`).

**Why not just subtract?** Naive template subtraction ŌĆö removing the estimated contamination from the data vector ŌĆö injects noise. The chi2 of the subtracted data, d'^T C^{-1} d', contains a X^T C^{-1} X term that dominates when templates are noisy (for ACT metacal: X^T C^{-1} X = 685 vs 2┬Ęd^T C^{-1} X = 266). This paradoxically *increased* the detection significance after "cleaning." Template marginalization avoids this entirely ŌĆö the Fisher matrix absorbs the template noise into the amplitude uncertainty rather than the data vector.
nodes.outcome: Template marginalization: ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06 (chi2/dof=0.75, PTE 0.98), 15Žā detection. SPT chi2/dof=1.6 persists ŌĆö deficit is CMB-side. Shown: lensmc ├Ś {ACT, SPT} before/after comparison plots.

nodes.title: Results table: paper-ready LaTeX compilation of all key numbers
nodes.body:
Compilation of all quantitative results into three paper-ready LaTeX tables. This is the tabular complement to the analysis summary figure ŌĆö where the figure tells the story visually, the tables provide the definitive reference numbers.

## Table 1: Detection & theory (Tab. 1 in paper)

One row per (method ├Ś CMB) combination (4 total). Columns progress through the analysis chain: raw Knox S/N ŌåÆ marginalized S/N ŌåÆ amplitude fits at three theory stages (vanilla Planck 2018, NLA IA, template marginalized) ŌåÆ joint chi2/PTE. The table shows how each correction stage affects the result: IA shifts ACT A_lens from 0.84ŌåÆ0.93, marginalization further to 0.86. SPT remains at 0.76 regardless ŌĆö the deficit is CMB-side.

Key numbers: ACT lensmc is the baseline (20Žā raw, 15Žā marginalized, A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06 final, chi2/dof=0.75 PTE 0.98). SPT detects at comparable raw significance (21Žā) but theory fails (chi2/dof=1.88, PTEŌēł0). metacal is consistent with lensmc across all quantities.

## Table 2: Per-bin breakdown (Tab. 2 in paper)

One row per tomographic bin (6 bins), lensmc only (metacal redundant at this detail level). Shows how detection significance rises from 5.5Žā (bin 1, z~0.3) to 11.6Žā (bin 6, z~1.5) ŌĆö consistent with increasing lensing kernel overlap at higher source redshift. ACT amplitude fits are consistent with unity in all bins (PTE > 0.1); SPT fails in bins 1-3 and 6, with bin 2 showing the worst deficit (A_lens=0.74, chi2/dof=2.6). The per-bin pattern suggests the SPT deficit is not confined to a single redshift, arguing against n(z) miscalibration as the sole explanation.

## Table 3: Validation summary (Tab. 3 in paper)

Nine tests organized by type: 4 consistency checks (all pass), 3 null tests (one sim false alarm at 1/66 = expected rate), 1 systematic null (16/70 fail from galactic extinction + stellar density), and the bias budget. The catalog vs map row uses Pearson correlation (r=0.998) instead of PTE, since the comparison is between two pipeline implementations rather than a null hypothesis.

The bias budget entry (max 4.4Žā stellar density, ACT) is the single most important validation concern ŌĆö stellar density contamination correlates with both shear and CMB ╬║, producing a harmonic-space bias exceeding the signal in bin 1. Template marginalization handles this at the likelihood level.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/generate_results_table.py`
nodes.outcome: Three LaTeX tables generated from evidence pipeline: (1) Detection & theory ŌĆö 4 method├ŚCMB combos with raw S/N (ACT 20Žā, SPT 21Žā), marginalized S/N (15Žā, 14Žā), amplitude fits (vanilla, IA, marginalized), and chi2/PTE. (2) Per-bin ŌĆö 6 bins with Knox S/N, NLA IA amplitude fits, and shape chi2 at best-fit A (╬Į=19 per bin, ╬Į=119 joint). ACT all pass shape, SPT bins 1-3 fail shape (chi2/╬Į 1.7-2.7) while bins 4-6 pass (1.2-1.6). All row consistent with Table 1 (╬Į=119 joint). (3) Validation ŌĆö 11 tests: 4 consistency pass, 3 null (jackknife, sim, spatial), systematic (16/70 fail), 2 Ōäō-range robustness (ACT 6/6 pass max 1.5Žā, SPT 5/6 max 2.7Žā bin 2), bias budget (max 4.4Žā). Iteration 32: Ōäō-range robustness integrated.

nodes.title: Ōäō-range robustness: ACT stable, SPT scale-dependent
nodes.body:
Progressively include bandpowers up to Ōäō_max and fit the lensing amplitude A_lens, testing whether the measurement is robust across angular scales. Starting from Ōäō_max Ōēł 105 (5 bandpowers minimum), each step adds one more bandpower until the full range (Ōäō Ōēł 2700).

**ACT is scale-stable.** Worst deviation from the full-range A_lens is 1.5Žā (bin 3). Bins 4-6 are rock-solid (< 1Žā). The ACT measurement is robust to Ōäō-range selection.

**SPT shows scale-dependent deficit.** Bins 1-3 start at A_lens Ōēł 1.5 with only low-Ōäō bandpowers and drop to A_lens Ōēł 0.7 as high-Ōäō data is added. The deficit is not a uniform calibration offset ŌĆö high-Ōäō bandpowers actively suppress the amplitude. Worst case: bin 2, max deviation 2.7Žā, A_lens range 0.74-2.55. Bins 4-6 are more stable (deviations 1.3-1.9Žā) but still trend downward.

This constrains the deficit's origin (full characterization in `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`). Three hypotheses, in order of likelihood:

1. **SPT ╬║ transfer function** ŌĆö residual Ōäō-dependent normalization in the Wiener-filtered ╬║ map, not captured by our flat theory scaling. CMB-side, SPT-specific. Favored: explains why ACT is clean.

2. **Higher-order noise bias leakage** ŌĆö N3/2 and trispectrum terms that formally cancel in cross-correlation but may leak at high Ōäō. Also CMB-side, SPT-specific.

3. **Baryonic feedback** ŌĆö suppresses small-scale matter power (Ōäō > 500-1000). Would match the downward trend but should affect both experiments equally. Disfavored by ACT stability.
nodes.outcome: ACT amplitude stable (worst deviation 1.5Žā, bin 3 lensmc). SPT shows significant scale dependence: worst 2.7Žā (bin 2 lensmc), driven by high-Ōäō bandpowers pulling amplitude DOWN. SPT bins 1-3 drop from A_lensŌēł1.5 at Ōäō_max~200 to A_lensŌēł0.7 at full range ŌĆö deficit is NOT a uniform calibration offset but scale-dependent, concentrated at high Ōäō.

nodes.title: The sky: ACT, SPT, and Euclid on the RR2 footprint
nodes.body:
Three maps, one footprint. The cross-correlation lives where CMB lensing convergence from space and ground overlaps with Euclid galaxy shapes. This figure shows what those fields look like on the sky ŌĆö the raw material before any spectrum is computed.

**ACT DR6 ╬║** covers a broad swath of southern sky (fsky Ōēł 0.25). The ILC lensing reconstruction is Wiener-filtered using Planck 2018 C_Ōäō^{╬║╬║} theory as the signal model ŌĆö the empirical approach fails because ACT's reconstruction noise is 25├Ś SPT's, leaving the auto-spectrum noise-dominated at all Ōäō (`wiener-filter-diagnostic-plot-w-14438464`). The theory-informed filter W(Ōäō) = C_theory / (C_theory + N_estimate) gives W < 0.5 everywhere but still reveals degree-scale convergence structure. Overlap with RR2: ~306 deg┬▓ (ACT Ōł® shear).

**SPT-3G winter ╬║** covers the deep winter field (Dec < -42┬░, fsky Ōēł 0.04). Only the southern patch of RR2 falls in this region. The deeper per-pixel noise gives Ōäō_{1/2} Ōēł 353 ŌĆö modes below this are signal-dominated. The GMV estimator combines temperature and polarization; TT, PP, and MV reconstructions provide systematic cross-checks (`tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`). Overlap with RR2: ~140 deg┬▓ (SPT Ōł® shear).

**Euclid RR2 shear weights** trace the galaxy density ŌĆö two disjoint patches at Dec Ōēł -33┬░ and Dec Ōēł -54┬░, with six tomographic bins slicing in redshift (`tapestry:compare_nz_distributions`).

All maps at nside 2048. The Wiener filter diagnostic (second plot) shows the power spectra, empirical and theory-informed filters side by side ŌĆö documenting why ACT needed a theory signal model.

**Scripts**: `workflow/scripts/plot_field_overview.py`, `workflow/scripts/plot_wiener_diagnostic.py`
nodes.outcome: Wiener-filtered CMB lensing convergence (ACT DR6, SPT-3G) and Euclid shear weights on the RR2 sky. Three fields, two projection patches, overlap areas for cross-correlation.

nodes.title: Apodization masks: what enters the cross-correlations
nodes.body:
Every NaMaster cross-spectrum multiplies two masks into the data before coupling matrix estimation. The CMB ╬║ side gets `nmt.mask_apodization(mask, 0.15, "C2")` ŌĆö a smooth cosine-squared taper that zeroes the field beyond the mask boundary. The shear side gets `apodize(weight > 0, 0.15, "C2") * weight` ŌĆö the same taper applied to the binary footprint, then multiplied by the galaxy weight map. The weight map encodes inverse shape-noise variance per pixel, so the effective mask is spatially varying even within the apodized boundary.

These masks define the effective sky fraction that enters the Knox covariance: `fsky_eff = Ō¤©wŌéüwŌééŌ¤®┬▓ / Ō¤©wŌéü┬▓wŌéé┬▓Ō¤®` (Hivon 2002). The C2 apodization with 0.15 rad (~8.6┬░) scale reduces fsky relative to the raw footprint but suppresses mode-coupling from sharp mask boundaries.

The figure shows raw vs apodized for all three masks: ACT, SPT, and shear weights. ACT's mask is broader (fsky Ōēł 0.25) and fills both RR2 patches. SPT's mask is compact (fsky Ōēł 0.04) and covers only the south patch. The shear weight map defines the Euclid galaxy density, with highest weights at the survey overlap centers and tapering at the edges.

Cross-ref: field overview (`tapestry:plot_field_overview`) shows the *data* on these masks. The `namaster-technical-patterns-60d42621` doc fiber documents the C2 apodization convention and its origin in gctools.
nodes.outcome: 24 tapestry nodes. Apodization masks visualized: ACT (fsky 0.248, 10225 deg┬▓), SPT (fsky 0.044, 1804 deg┬▓), shear (fsky 0.009, 385 deg┬▓). C2 apodization at 0.15 rad preserves mask area ŌĆö ACT mean weight drops only 0.4%. Shear weights multiplied after apodization (apodize(w>0) ├Ś w). Plot shows raw vs apodized for both RR2 patches.

nodes.title: Systematic maps on the sky: stellar density and galactic extinction on RR2
nodes.body:
Stellar density and galactic extinction are the two systematics that matter. The bias budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) shows stellar density dominates ACT contamination (S_bias up to 8Žā for metacal), with galactic extinction second (up to 5.8Žā). Exposures, SAA, and zodiacal light are clean (chi2/dof Ōēł 1). This figure shows where the contamination lives spatially.

Both maps come from the Euclid VMPZ-ID mosaic masks pipeline (sparse HEALPix at nside 16384, NESTED ordering). We load and downgrade to nside 2048 using `read_partial_map`, zeroing HEALPix UNSEEN sentinel values that otherwise dominate cross-spectra.

Stellar density traces Milky Way structure ŌĆö higher toward the galactic plane, with gradients across the two RR2 patches. Galactic extinction follows a similar but not identical pattern, with additional structure from dust filaments. Both systematics contaminate cross-spectra because they correlate with both the shear field (through spatially-varying galaxy selection) and the CMB ╬║ field (through the ISW effect and residual foreground leakage in the lensing reconstruction).

The contamination metric `X^f_S(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S} ┬Ę C^{fS} / C^{SS}` (`tapestry:plot_contamination_metric`) quantifies the per-bandpower bias from each systematic. Template marginalization (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`) removes their effect at the cost of ~5Žā in detection significance (20Žā ŌåÆ 15Žā for ACT).

Cross-ref: masks on which these maps are evaluated (`tapestry:plot_apodization_masks`).
nodes.outcome: Stellar density and galactic extinction visualized on RR2 footprint. Stellar density: median 0 (sparse), p90 0.1 stars/deg┬▓. Galactic extinction: median E(B-V) 0.016, p90 0.030. Both loaded from VMPZ-ID nside 16384 sparse FITS via read_partial_map, downgraded to 2048. These are the two dominant contaminants identified by the bias budget (S_bias up to 8Žā for stellar density, 5.8Žā for galactic extinction in ACT).

fibers.title: TR1 B-mode investigation
fibers.outcome:
## Status: No smoking gun ŌĆö tiger team being formed

B-modes are significant in TR1. Bologna meeting (Feb 2026) exhausted all obvious avenues without identifying source. Benjamin to captain a dedicated tiger team.

## What was tried

### Nicholas de Sore ŌĆö Cl cuts
- Split by faint/bright, large/small objects
- "Interesting things but nothing conclusive"

### 2D mass maps
- Benjamin: "might be a hint of structure" but no smoking gun

### Angus ŌĆö 2PCF vs CosmoPipe comparison
- Significant B-modes regardless of method (L3 2PCF or KiDS CosmoPipe)
- **Discrepancy between L3 and CosmoPipe on some scales** ŌĆö being investigated
- Angus provided KiDS data to cross-check; expected to be a fixable issue
- Masking tests: effective coverage mask (>0.8) removes ~couple orders of B-mode but still significant at ~1e-9. Cluster-based mask also doesn't eliminate.

### Chris Duncan ŌĆö PSF size residuals
- Useful diagnostic but not clearly the source
- PSF looks good on small scales ŌĆö confusing why B-modes appear on large scales
- PSF has been improved since R2 but large-scale B-modes haven't changed much

### Sasha ŌĆö rho/tau statistics
- Various patches, faint/bright cuts
- UCL team selected patches avoiding edge/overlap complications
- **PSF appears to be behaving fairly well** ŌĆö no obvious PSF-galaxy correlation

### Lila ŌĆö C-bias residual patterns on sky
- Initially noticed funny patterns
- Resolved as projection issue (detector ŌåÆ sky coordinates) with Gordon and Chris
- Redmine issue open for documentation

### Leonor (UCL) ŌĆö Average ellipticity in clean patches
- Some odd behavior in a few patches
- Not clearly connected to B-modes

### Angus ŌĆö SOM correlations
- Self-organizing maps looking for ellipticity correlations with survey parameters
- Nothing clear; more detailed analysis planned

## Key discussion points
- **Simulations**: "If only we had simulations" ŌĆö DPS downtime adds ~1 month delay. Nico clarified: never planned to do 100 sq deg sims, only 32 small patches across DR1. Full survey sims could be prioritized but pipeline is late.
- **PSF propagation**: Would be useful to propagate PSF size biases to see if they produce large-scale B-modes, but no simulation infrastructure for this.
- **Alan**: Offered to run data through ALMANAC (Bayesian B-mode power spectrum sampler) ŌĆö to coordinate with Benjamin/Sam/Angus.
- **Masking + C-bias recomputation**: Angus plans to recompute C-term after masking selections (now has Jacobians from CosmoHub catalog). Doubts it will change C-term significantly, but wants to test C-term vs brightness etc.

## Assessment
Source unknown. Could be PSF-related, could be something else entirely. "They can hide very well and we have to dig deeper" (Henry). Cannot categorically rule out going "all the way back" to fundamentals.

fibers.title: DPS downtime ŌĆö simulation delay
fibers.outcome: DPS down >1 month. ~1 month delay to sim production. ESA aware. Nico: 100 sq deg sims were never planned ŌĆö only 32 small patches across DR1 for survey environment variability. Full-survey sims (needed for propagating PSF biases ŌåÆ large-scale B-modes) not in scope. Could be prioritized but pipeline already late. Simulation gap is a bottleneck for B-mode diagnosis.

fibers.title: CMBX 3x2pt blinding strategy
fibers.body:
CMBX 3x2pt blinded consistently with main Euclid 3x2pt blinding. Lets us compare results without unblinding and simplifies collaboration across working groups.

Done with support from Sam Farrens but outside the official Euclid 3x2pt blinding framework. We match the JC1 scheme as closely as possible: a list of cosmologies generates corresponding data-vector shifts, and one shift is randomly picked by an external blinder (Sam Farrens or Chaitanya Chawak).

## Consistency Requirements

To stay consistent with 3x2pt, we need:

1. The same cosmology list used in JC1 to produce our shifts
2. Verification that our shifts match CLOE's for the common data vectors:
   - Galaxy clustering (dd)
   - Weak lensing (gg)
   - Galaxy-galaxy lensing (dg)

This verification is our responsibility.

## Workflow

1. Produce unblinded data vectors from catalogs on the SAS
2. Run basic numerical checks for pathologies ŌĆö never plot them
3. Submit via Redmine to Sam Farrens and Chaitanya Chawak:
   - Unblinded data vectors
   - Our computed shifts
   - Mixing matrices
4. Receive blinded vectors back

The mixing matrix must be applied after blinding. Alternatively, we could pass pre-mixed blinded vectors instead of the unblinded vector, shifts, and matrix separately.

## The Blinding Transformation

Following the JC1 scheme, blinding applies a shift at the level of summary statistics:

C_l^blind = C_l^obs + DC_l

where DC_l = C_l(theta_ref + Dtheta) - C_l(theta_ref)

- theta_ref: reference (fiducial) cosmology
- Dtheta: blind parameter shift, drawn from a predefined list
- The reference cosmology should describe the data well
- The blinded result remains consistent with a realization of theta_ref + Dtheta

One shift is selected at random by the external blinder from the predefined list.

## Data Format Specification

Following the DPDD 2PCF format for Euclid's 3x2pt data products, extended for CMBX cross-correlations.

### Data Vector Components

| Component | DPDD Product | Spectra (6 bins) |
|-----------|-------------|------------------|
| dd | TwoPCFWLClPosPos2D | 21 (6 auto + 15 cross) |
| gg | TwoPCFWLPEBShearShear2D | 21 (6 auto + 15 cross) |
| dg | TwoPCFWLPEBPosShear2D | 36 (6x6) |
| dk | (extension) | 6 |
| gk | (extension) | 6 |
| kk | (extension) | 1 |
| **Total** | | **91 spectra** |

With ~20 l-bins per spectrum: ~1820 data points.

### FITS Structure

Each data product follows the DPDD 2PCF structure:
- Primary HDU: Metadata keywords including BLINDING flag, SOFTNAME, SOFTVERS, angular binning parameters
- Extension HDUs: One per bin combination, named {TYPE}_{n}
- HDU headers: BIN_ID1, BIN_ID2 specify the tomographic bin pair

Standard columns: ELL, ELL_LOWER, ELL_UPPER

### CMBX Extensions

For CMB lensing cross-correlations (dk, gk, kk):
- HDU naming: POSKAPPA_CL_{n}, SHEARKAPPA_PEB_{n}, KAPPAKAPPA_CL_1
- CMB lensing has no tomography: use BIN_ID2=0 for k
- Follow same column structure as corresponding Euclid products

### Shift Vectors

Match the data vector structure exactly. Multiple shift files: shifts_001.fits, shifts_002.fits, etc.

## Implementation Status

A prototype shift-generation script exists. It computes DC_l = -C_l^bandpower(theta_ref) + C_l^bandpower(theta_shift) and saves to timestamped pickle files. Output format will be converted to DPDD-aligned FITS.

## Open Items

- Obtain cosmology parameter list from JC1 (Martin Crocce / Guadalupe)
- Establish Redmine issue template for shift submission
- Confirm timeline with Sam Farrens
- Validate theory code agreement with CLOE on common vectors

## References

- Muir et al. 2019 (arXiv:1911.05929): Blinding multi-probe cosmological experiments
- Euclid DPDD: http://st-dm.pages.euclid-sgs.uk/data-product-doc/dmdr1/
- JC1 blinding slides
fibers.outcome: Settled: fixed-cosmology A_lens fitting only. No Omega_m, S_8, or richer parameter fits. Current analysis is internal ŌĆö don't share broadly. The full 3x2pt blinding protocol (DPDD format, shift vectors, Redmine submission to Sam Farrens) remains documented here for when we move to parameter inference, but that's not TR1 scope.

fibers.title: CMBX validation test proposals
fibers.body:
# 2025-06-25: Validation Test Proposals

With work progressing RR2, we thought it would be a good time to start defining a set of consistency checks, null tests, conditional tests, etc. that will eventually be used for DR1. We have taken inspiration from the tests performed in the [DES x SPT](https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.12440) and Q1 cross-correlation analyses.

## Q1 tests

1. Building jackknives maps from random split of the RR2 sample (null galaxy clustering signal maps) and use them as signal to compute their auto-correlation and CMB lensing cross-correlation.
2. Cross-correlation between RR2 sample and CMB lensing maps for which we expect to have no or detectable cosmological signal (ACT curl mode map or ACT diff 150GHz - 90 GHz)
3. Cross-correlation of RR2 sample with CMB lensing maps derived with different type of lensing reconstruction estimators that have different sensitivity to Galactic and extragalactic foregrounds.

[Slides from Giulio on Q1 validation tests](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1sxCQnp2zRyyd5Zz-Si0FJz7kpVjmUlOjq1iaQYkstO0/edit?slide=id.g36c87a7ff49_0_50#slide=id.g36c87a7ff49_0_50)

## DES x SPT tests

1. **Survey property (i.e. systematic) map cross-correlations.**
   If we are worried about a given systematic affecting both tracers and thus contributing to the cross-correlation, can make a map that probes this systematic and cross correlate it with each tracer, and potentially deproject a systematics template if the contamination is significant. In the DES x SPT analysis, they do make survey property maps of stellar density, extinction, PSF ellipticity errors, and PSF size errors.

2. **Cross-shear gamma_x.**
   In the DES x SPT analysis the shear catalog is cross-correlated directly with the CMB lensing map without producing an intermediate shear map. This is done by taking the tangential component of each galaxy's ellipticity relative to each kappa_CMB pixel to form <gamma_t kappa_CMB>. If instead the component of the galaxy shear oriented 45┬║ relative to each kappa_CMB pixel is taken one can form <gamma_x kappa_CMB> which should be consistent with zero. At least until lensing curl from post-Born effects is detectable.

3. **Effect of delta_g weights on <delta_g kappa_CMB>.**
   Application of galaxy position weights is intended to reduce systematics, so this isn't exactly a null test. But performing the cross-correlation with and without the weights can give a sense of how big the weight correction is, and can be propagated to cosmological parameters.

4. **Point-source masking in kappa_CMB.**
   To make sure the inpainting of point sources in the CMB temperature maps is not biasing the lensing reconstruction, they try simply masking these point sources instead. Not clear if they mask the original CMB maps, or just the lensing construction.

5. **Spatial variations in kappa_CMB.**
   They split their kappa_CMB maps into northern and southern patches, and then cross-correlate both with an external tracer (e.g. CIB). The two cross-correlations should be consistent. This is mostly a test of the lensing maps, but if you saw inconsistency between spatial splits of <gamma_t kappa_CMB> or <delta_g kappa_CMB>, this would help tease out whether the problem is on the CMB or galaxy side.

## Other

1. Sample selection cuts (magnitude, shear weights, etc.). Will require recalibration of shear?
2. Consistency between tracers:
   - kappa_CMB: ACT vs. SPT vs. Planck, MV vs Pol reconstruction, bias hardening...
   - gamma: Metacal vs. Lensmc
   - delta_g: only one tracer?
3. Null shear & galaxy maps

## RR2 validation tests proposal [16 July 2025]

Linked to task #31817

Following Q1 analysis, we can perform catalog based, spatial based, and CMB lensing null tests and validation. The tests we can do for RR2 depends on the availability of external data (for CMB lensing side), Euclid data and correlated simulations. We do not plan to have correlated simulations with foreground contaminations for RR2 but that is something we should aim for DR1 (eg Agora sims).

1. Cross correlation with jackknives maps from random split of the RR2 sample, nulling galaxy clustering signal
2. Cross correlation with CMB lensing maps with no signal (curl map, frequency difference maps)
   - TODO: list available lensing maps for Planck, ACT and SPT
3. Cross correlation with CMB lensing maps obtained with different estimators (tSZ cleaned leg in the quadratic estimator, different masking, T only versus Pol only versus MV)
   - TODO: list available lensing maps for Planck, ACT and SPT
4. Check consistency of cross-correlations with Planck vs ACT vs SPT
5. Survey property (i.e. systematic) map cross-correlations, following DESxSPT analysis, needs:
   - TODO: get extinction and stellar density maps (from Gaia data, or Euclid internal)
   - TODO: PSF error map
6. Cross-shear gamma_x
   - TODO: this test is designed in real space, check if correspondence in multipole space is to use mode B lensing maps
7. Spatial variation: In RR2 we have two patches in the south only. We can further divide the large one into two smaller patches
fibers.outcome: Implementation status of proposed validation tests: (1) Jackknife random split ŌĆö DONE, 24/24 pass (iteration 8). (2) Curl/frequency-diff CMB maps ŌĆö BLOCKED, ACT has only baseline ILC kappa, SPT has estimator variants but no curl. Would need ACT collaboration request. (3) Different CMB estimators ŌĆö DONE, SPT TT/PP/MV all consistent with GMV (iteration 6). (4) ACT vs SPT consistency ŌĆö DONE, 14/14 pass at 5% (iteration 4). (5) Systematic map cross-correlations ŌĆö DONE, 70 tests + contamination metric X + bias budget (iterations 1-20). Galactic extinction and stellar density contaminate ACT. (6) Cross-shear gamma_x ŌĆö NOT DONE, EŌåÆB leakage at fsky~0.1 makes B-mode cross-spectrum unreliable as null test. (7) Spatial variation ŌĆö DONE, north/south 2-patch split, 6/6 pass at ell>100 (iteration 18). (8) Simulation null ŌĆö DONE (bonus), 11 SPT sims ├Ś 6 bins, 1/66 false alarm (iteration 17). Additionally: theory comparison with NLA IA, template marginalization, fiducial likelihood evaluation, results tables all completed. Remaining gaps: curl maps (blocked on data), cross-shear (blocked on E/B leakage physics), Planck kappa (not prioritized).

fibers.title: The data: 6 redshift bins across the RR2 footprint
fibers.body:
The redshift dimension. For what the fields look like on the sky, see `tapestry:plot_field_overview`. This node shows how those galaxies are distributed in redshift ŌĆö the tomographic binning that gives the cross-correlation its depth information.

**Six bins from z Ōēł 0.2 to 2.0.** The Euclid RR2 v2.1 weak lensing catalog contains ~50 million galaxies with shape measurements from two independent methods (lensmc and metacal). Tomographic bins are defined by the photometric pipeline, with n(z) distributions from `RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits`. The effective sky fraction is ~1% per bin at nside 2048.

**CMB side.** ACT DR6 (ILC reconstruction) and SPT-3G winter (GMV + TT/PP/MV variants). ACT overlaps the full Euclid footprint; SPT covers only the southern patch (Dec < -42┬░).

**28 baseline spectra.** C_Ōäō^{╬║E} in 20 log-spaced bandpowers (Ōäō = 40ŌĆō3000): 2 methods ├Ś 7 bins ├Ś 2 CMB experiments. These are the inputs to everything downstream ŌĆö the signal overview (`tapestry:plot_signal_overview`), consistency tests, null tests, and theory comparison.

**Covariance.** Knox formula throughout: Žā┬▓(C_b) = C^{╬║╬║}_b ┬Ę C^{╬│╬│}_b / n_modes, where n_modes = ╬Ż(2Ōäō+1) ┬Ę f_sky^eff. The effective sky fraction f_sky^eff = Ō¤©wŌéüwŌééŌ¤®┬▓ / Ō¤©wŌéü┬▓wŌéé┬▓Ō¤® (Hivon 2002) accounts for the apodized weight maps. Auto-spectra C^{╬║╬║} and C^{╬│╬│} are computed from the masked maps (not catalog-based). This is the diagonal of the covariance ŌĆö no off-diagonal (bin-bin) correlations. Knox is preferred over NaMaster Gaussian, which overestimates variance by 10-130├Ś at our fsky.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/compare_nz_distributions.py`

## Comments
**2026-02-15 23:44** ŌĆö n(z) plot needs updating: (1) Plot the actual shear n(z) from the 6-bin file (RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits), not the forecasted version. May need smoothing. (2) Empirical check: take bin IDs from the catalog and plot by photo-z median ŌĆö verify bins match the n(z) file. (3) Galaxy clustering n(z) is optional for now, stick with shear.
fibers.outcome: Euclid RR2 v2.1 weak lensing catalog: ~50M galaxies, 6 tomographic bins, nside 2048. n(z) from theoretical pipeline (RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits). Effective sky fraction ~0.01 per bin.

fibers.title: RR2 v1.4 dataset creation
fibers.body:
## Date

2025-09-18

## Dataset Location

`/leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083/inputs/euclid/rr2/catalogs/rr2_v1_4_250918/`

## Catalog Updates

Enhanced sample selection criteria implemented via cosmohub query on `euclid_rr2_r1_v1_4` table (7.3GB parquet):

### Core Quality Selection

- `spurious_flag = 0` - Removes spurious detections
- `vis_det = 1` - VIS detection required
- `det_quality_flag & ~(1|2|512) = 0` - Excludes specific quality flags via bitwise operation
- `phz_flags = 0` - Clean photometric redshift measurements
- `tom_bin_id != -1` - Valid tomographic bin assignment
- `eff_cov_flag > 0` - Effective coverage requirement
- `point_like_prob < 0.1` - Excludes point sources

### Enhanced NaN Filtering

Implemented explicit checks ensuring both weight > 0 AND measurements exist:

```sql
(she_lensmc_weight > 0 AND she_lensmc_e1_corrected IS NOT NULL AND she_lensmc_e1_corrected IS NOT NULL)
OR (she_metacal_weight > 0 AND she_metacal_e1_corrected IS NOT NULL AND she_metacal_e2_corrected IS NOT NULL)
```

### New Analysis Columns Added

- **Size vs SNR**: `she_lensmc_snr`, `flux_vis_2fwhm_aper/fluxerr_vis_2fwhm_aper`, `she_metacal_sn`
- **PSF size ratios**: `she_lensmc_psf_r2`, `she_metacal_psf_r2`
- **Flux measurements**: `mag_stargal_sep`, `flux_vis_psf`, `she_lensmc_flux`, `she_lensmc_mag`

Note: `flux_vis_unif` cuts (1.44544 to 36.30781) moved to post-processing for analysis flexibility.

## Supporting Data Products

### Masks and Coverage

- `coverage_mask.fits` (132MB) - RR2 survey coverage mask
- `visibility_mask.fits` (51MB) - VIS instrument visibility constraints
- Associated README files with detailed specifications

### Mass Maps for Cross-Correlation

- `SKS_mass_map.fits` (3.3GB) - Full-sphere Kaiser-Squires mass reconstruction
- `SKSP_mass_map.fits` (3.3GB) - Full-sphere KS with inpainting (KSp method)
  - Each contains: 1 FITS extension for map + 1 extension for mask
  - Provides convergence fields for ╬║╬│ and ╬║╬║ cross-correlation analysis

### Redshift Distributions

- `nz_files.fits` (79KB) - n(z) for tomographic bins, essential for theoretical modeling

## Key Improvements Over v1_250616

1. **Enhanced quality cuts**: More robust selection against systematics
2. **Explicit NaN handling**: Prevents analysis failures from missing shear measurements
3. **Extended column set**: Enables size-SNR-PSF analysis for systematic studies
4. **Mass map integration**: Direct access to convergence fields for expanded analysis scope
5. **Complete documentation**: Query provenance and parameter documentation preserved

## Analysis Readiness

This dataset is production-ready for:

- **Cross-correlation analysis**: Enhanced catalog quality + direct mass map access
- **Systematic studies**: Size/SNR/PSF columns enable detailed systematic characterization
- **Theoretical comparison**: Complete n(z) information for accurate theoretical predictions
- **Multi-probe analysis**: Integration of galaxy catalog + mass maps + masks for comprehensive analysis

## Status Note

This dataset was superseded by RR2 v2.1 in later development. It served as the foundation for establishing the full analysis pipeline (catalog ŌåÆ maps ŌåÆ cross-spectra ŌåÆ SACC) documented in the 2025-10-06 pipeline inventory.
fibers.outcome: Created RR2 v1.4 dataset with enhanced quality cuts, explicit NaN filtering, and extended analysis columns. 7.3GB catalog plus masks and mass maps. Production-ready for cross-correlation analysis. Superseded by v2.1.

fibers.title: Cross-correlation pipeline v2: NaMaster patterns from gctools
fibers.body:
Euclid ├Ś CMB-╬║ cross-correlation pipeline. The infrastructure works (rules, wildcards, packaging, plots). The spectra are wrong ŌĆö all negative where they should be positive.

## Desired State

A working, robust cross-correlation pipeline where every detail is either addressed or raised as an open fiber. By comparing against gctools (map-based, working) and sp_validation (catalog-based, reference), converge on an implementation that produces correct, physically sensible spectra. Make as much progress as possible each iteration ŌĆö fix what you can, file what you can't.

`workflow/scripts/compute_cross_spectrum.py` produces cross-spectra that:

1. **Start from gctools conventions.** When in doubt, match `spectra.py::map2wlcls` ŌĆö it's a working implementation that produces correct results. Divergences from gctools are the first place to look when debugging. Once we have broad agreement with Margherita's results (positive C^{E╬║}, sensible shape and amplitude), we can improve: better noise handling, different binning, etc. The point isn't to clone gctools forever ŌĆö it's to have a known-good baseline to build from.

2. **Produce positive C^{E╬║}_Ōäō.** The E-mode shear ├Ś CMB convergence cross-spectrum traces the same large-scale matter distribution. It must be positive, with amplitude ~10Ōü╗┬╣Ōü░. If it's negative, something is wrong ŌĆö don't proceed to other work until this is resolved.

3. **Have a resolved sign convention** for the e1/e2 ŌåÆ Q/U mapping, documented in a decision fiber with reasoning. `sign_test_lean.py` tests all 4 combinations on real data; run it if the sign isn't resolved after matching gctools.

4. **Use shape noise from FITS headers** for the shear noise power spectrum. Each shear map header contains `NOISE_CL` (= Žā┬▓_comp / n_eff_sr), the flat per-component shape noise CŌäō. Use this as `nl_uncoupled` for WL fields ŌĆö it replaces Margherita's `None` (which she marked as a TODO) and the BBŌłÆEE subtraction trick. For cross-spectra with CMB ╬║ there's no noise bias to subtract (independent experiments), but the auto-spectra fed into the Gaussian covariance should include this noise.

5. **Use iNKA Gaussian covariance** following `spectra.py::get_cov`. Input spectra are coupled auto/cross spectra normalized by mean(mask_i ├Ś mask_j). Spins detected from field type. This is the working pattern ŌĆö don't introduce theory-based covariance yet.

6. **Support catalog-based cross-spectra.** The shear tracer can be constructed directly from the parquet catalog as an `NmtFieldCatalog` (spin-2), crossed with the CMB ╬║ `NmtField` (spin-0). NaMaster handles mixed field types transparently ŌĆö `compute_coupled_cell` and workspace creation work across NmtFieldCatalog ├Ś NmtField as long as lmax matches. This bypasses the map-making step entirely, providing an independent validation path. The catalog-based and map-based cross-spectra should agree; disagreement isolates bugs in map construction vs. spectrum computation.

   The catalog preparation mirrors `build_maps.py`: load the parquet, filter `tom_bin_id == bin` and `she_{method}_weight > 0`, exclude bad tiles (from config), subtract weighted-mean additive bias (c1, c2). Pass `[ra, dec]` as positions (lonlat=True), weights, and `[e1, ┬▒e2]` as fields to `NmtFieldCatalog`. nx2pt already has `CatalogTracer` (`code/nx2pt/src/nx2pt/tracer.py` line 69) that wraps this.

**Done when:**
- `C^{E╬║}_Ōäō` is positive for lensmc ├Ś ACT (the baseline case)
- Cross-spectrum shape is physically sensible (positive, decaying at high Ōäō)
- Map-based and catalog-based cross-spectra agree (at least for one bin ├Ś CMB experiment)
- Sign convention is documented in a closed decision fiber
- Systematic null tests re-run and PTEs are reasonable (not all > 0.8)
- Check: `snakemake -n compute_all_shear_cross_spectra` is clean

## Context

### gctools (authoritative)

`code/dr1_cmbx/src/dr1_cmbx/eDR1data/gctools/spectra.py` ŌĆö the working implementation:
- `map2wlcls()` (line 708): field creation, masking, spectrum computation for WL ├Ś CMB-╬║
- `get_cl()` (line 13): coupled ŌåÆ decoupled spectrum, optional noise subtraction via `pnl`
- `get_cov()` (line 135): iNKA Gaussian covariance, spin-aware
- `get_spectra()` (line 93): orchestrates fields ŌåÆ spectra, passes `nl_uncoupled` for auto-spectra only

### sp_validation (reference)

`../sp_validation/src/sp_validation/cosmo_val.py` ŌĆö catalog-based pseudo-CŌäō reference from CosmoStat. Shows the full NaMaster pattern including `NmtFieldCatalog`, analytic noise bias, and theory+noise covariance. Useful for understanding the general pattern; we don't need to match it exactly.

### Shear map noise metadata

FITS headers on `rr2_v2_1_wl_031224_method=lensmc_bin={n}_shear_maps.fits` contain:
```
NOISE_CL   ŌĆö flat shape noise CŌäō (Žā┬▓_comp / n_eff_sr)
SIG_EPS    ŌĆö total ellipticity dispersion
SIG_COMP   ŌĆö per-component dispersion (Žā_╬Ą / ŌłÜ2)
NEFF_SR    ŌĆö effective galaxy density [srŌü╗┬╣]
```

### Catalog-based pseudo-CŌäō

`NmtFieldCatalog` takes positions, weights, and field values directly ŌĆö no map-making. For shear ├Ś CMB-╬║, the shear side is catalog-based and the CMB side is map-based. NaMaster's `is_compatible()` only checks harmonic resolution (`ainfo.lmax`), not field type, so mixed cross-spectra work transparently.

**Catalog**: `/leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083/cmbx/inputs/euclid/catalogs/rr2_v2_1_wl_031224/rr2_v2_1_wl_031224.parquet` (~30M galaxies). Columns needed: `right_ascension`, `declination`, `she_{method}_e1_corrected`, `she_{method}_e2_corrected`, `she_{method}_weight`, `tom_bin_id`, `tile_index`.

**Cuts** (same as `build_maps.py`): `tom_bin_id == bin`, `weight > 0`, bad tiles from config, then subtract weighted-mean c1/c2.

**nx2pt CatalogTracer**: `code/nx2pt/src/nx2pt/tracer.py` line 69 ŌĆö takes `pos=[ra,dec]`, `weights`, `fields=[e1, e2]`, `lmax`, `spin=2`, `lonlat=True`. Creates `NmtFieldCatalog` internally.

**sp_validation reference**: `../sp_validation/src/sp_validation/cosmo_val.py` ŌĆö shows `NmtFieldCatalog` usage with `pol_factor` toggle for sign convention testing, analytic noise bias, theory+noise covariance.

### Our pipeline

- Map-based script: `workflow/scripts/compute_cross_spectrum.py`
- Map builder: `workflow/scripts/build_maps.py` (catalog ŌåÆ sparse FITS maps)
- Systematics: `workflow/scripts/compute_systematic_cross_spectrum.py`
- Rules: `workflow/Snakefile` + `workflow/rules/explorations.smk`
- Sign test: `workflow/scripts/sign_test_lean.py`
- nx2pt workspace caching: `code/nx2pt/src/nx2pt/namaster_tools.py`

### Data

- Shear catalog: `/leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083/cmbx/inputs/euclid/catalogs/rr2_v2_1_wl_031224/rr2_v2_1_wl_031224.parquet`
- Shear maps: `/leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083/cmbx/inputs/euclid/maps/rr2_v2_1_wl_031224/wl_{lensmc,metacal}/`
- ACT ╬║ + mask: `inputs/act/` (mask in `inputs/act/masks/`)
- SPT ╬║ + mask: `inputs/spt/maps/lensing/winter/gmv/` + `inputs/spt/masks/lensing/winter/`

### Related fibers

- `shear-e1-e2-healpix-q-u-sign-ec695758` ŌĆö sign convention investigation
- `validation-negative-cross-40ecf87e` ŌĆö negative spectra tracking
- `suspiciously-good-ptes-in-dbf44cbb` ŌĆö PTE investigation (likely resolves after spectra fix)

## Skills

- `/snakemake` ŌĆö workflow design and execution
- `/implementing-code` ŌĆö script development
- `/data-visualization` ŌĆö plotting

## Comments
**2026-02-14 04:36** ŌĆö Spec reworked (2026-02-14). Key findings from review: (1) CMB mask discrepancy ŌĆö we square, gctools apodizes. Confirmed: switch to apodize. (2) Binning: switch to linear nlb=100 to match gctools for comparison. (3) NOISE_CL in FITS headers provides clean shape noise NŌäō for Phase 2. (4) sp_validation cloned to ../sp_validation/ as catalog pseudo-CŌäō reference.
fibers.outcome:
Pipeline complete. All six done-when criteria met:

(1) C^{E╬║} positive ŌĆö 28/28 cross-spectra (lensmc+metacal ├Ś 7bins ├Ś ACT+SPT) are positive with amplitude ~10Ōü╗ŌüČ in ŌäōCŌäō.

(2) Physically sensible shape ŌĆö signal peaks at low Ōäō, decays at high Ōäō, increases with redshift bin as expected from lensing geometry. B-modes consistent with noise.

(3) Map-based and catalog-based agree ŌĆö 28/28 catalog (NmtFieldCatalog) vs map cross-spectra match with correlation 0.986-1.000, fractional differences <10% where S/N>1.

(4) Sign convention resolved ŌĆö [-e1, e2] (standard WL), documented in sign-convention-for-standard-wl-7ebc8d73.

(5) Systematic null tests clean ŌĆö Knox covariance gives chi2/dof~1 for exposures, SAA, zodiacal light. Galactic extinction contaminates (chi2/dof 1.3-9.0, tracked in galactic-extinction-ecfc9972). Stellar density moderate.

(6) DAG clean ŌĆö snakemake -n returns nothing-to-be-done for all shear/systematic/catalog/plot targets.

Signal detection: 28/28 at 3Žā, 27/28 at 5Žā (Knox). Median SNR=8.2, max=11.75 (lensmc bin6 ├Ś SPT). SACC files produced for ACT and SPT-3G. 42 downstream fibers document every decision, bug, and pattern encountered.

Open items tracked in separate fibers: mass maps (mass-maps-at-v1-4-path-not-v2-1-fdc14589), galactic extinction mitigation (galactic-extinction-ecfc9972), blinding (cmbx-3x2pt-blinding-strategy-d2469de7).

fibers.title: First systematic null test (stellar density ├Ś lensmc bin1)
fibers.body:
Cross-spectra between Euclid tracer maps and systematic template maps, testing whether observational systematics leak into the shear signal. Produces 70 spectra: 5 systematics ├Ś 2 methods ├Ś 7 bins.

**Systematic maps.** Five VMPZ-ID templates at nside 16384 (NESTED): stellar density, galactic extinction, number of exposures, SAA passages, zodiacal light. Loaded via `dr1_cmbx.eDR1data.gctools.reader.read_partial_map()` which handles sparse FITS and downgrading to the analysis nside. Critical: HP UNSEEN sentinels (|val| > 1e20) must be zeroed after loading ŌĆö they dominate the power spectrum if left in.

**Mask.** The analysis mask is the intersection of the survey footprint and the systematic map's coverage (non-zero pixels). Both apodized following the standard convention.

**Null hypothesis.** Under H0, the cross-spectrum C^{fS}_Ōäō is consistent with zero ŌĆö systematics should not correlate with shear. Knox chi2/dof ~ 1 for clean systematics. Galactic extinction is the exception: chi2/dof 1.3-9.0 across bins, with strongest contamination in high-z bins (5, 6). This is real astrophysical contamination, not a pipeline artifact.

**Downstream.** These spectra feed into the contamination metric (via `plot_contamination_metric`) and the summary heatmap (via `plot_systematic_null_tests`). The C^{SS} auto-spectrum (stored as `cl_sys_auto` in each NPZ) is needed for the full DES├ŚSPT-style X metric.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/compute_systematic_cross_spectrum.py`
fibers.outcome: stellar_density ├Ś lensmc bin1: chi2=1.7 (dof=20, PTE=1.0). Consistent with zero ŌĆö no contamination. read_partial_map from gctools handles sparse FITS + nside downgrade efficiently. Full batch of 70 jobs launched.

fibers.title: Claims evidence directory structure
fibers.outcome: Dashboard reads results/claims/<rule>/evidence.json for per-rule summaries. Individual per-wildcard evidence stays alongside NPZ outputs. aggregate_evidence.py + Snakefile rules produce the summaries. One claim fiber per rule: tag.
