fibers.outcome: Added localrules declarations to Snakefile (target/aggregator rules) and explorations.smk (plot rules). Plot localrules run via apptainer on login node ŌĆö slow (~1 min/plot due to container startup) but functional. 28 cross-spectrum plots generated successfully.

fibers.title: Galactic extinction FITS truncated ŌĆö swapped to 131741 version
fibers.outcome: The 409M galactic_extinction file (131145 timestamp) was truncated, causing FITSIO read errors. Swapped config to point to the 648M version (131741 timestamp), which reads all 42M rows successfully. All 14 galactic_extinction x shear null tests now pass (PTE ~1.0).

fibers.title: SACC combine uses Misc tracers ŌĆö needs NZ upgrade
fibers.body:
Packages 28 individual cross-spectrum NPZ files into a single SACC file per CMB experiment, ready for downstream likelihood analysis. SACC (Save All Cross-Correlations) is the standard Euclid/LSST data vector format ŌĆö the interface between spectrum estimation and parameter inference.

**Why SACC.** SACC files carry not just data vectors but also tracer metadata (n(z), beam functions), bandpower definitions, and covariance blocks. This is what the eDR1like likelihood reads. The format enables sharing spectra with the wider Euclid/LSST community and ensures that all information needed to compute theory predictions is bundled with the data.

**Tracers.** 14 shear tracers as NZTracer (z, n(z) from `RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits`, 6 bins + "all" bin ├Ś 2 methods). 1 CMB ╬║ tracer as MiscTracer (no n(z) ŌĆö CMB lensing has a broad kernel computed internally by the theory code). Total: 15 tracers per file. Originally used MiscTracer for shear (with no n(z)), upgraded to NZTracer when likelihood integration required redshift distributions.

**Data vector.** 560 data points per SACC file (14 cross-spectra ├Ś 40 bandpowers each ŌĆö 20 E-mode + 20 B-mode). 20 log-spaced bins from Ōäō=40 to Ōäō=3000 (decision: `bandpower-binning-20-log-spaced-3628f69e`).

**Covariance.** Block diagonal Knox covariance included. Off-diagonal blocks (cross-bin, cross-method correlations) not computed ŌĆö this is a limitation for joint inference but sufficient for per-spectrum significance tests. The full joint covariance used by `evaluate_fiducial_likelihood` comes from the PKL files (`build_likelihood_input`), not from SACC.

**Downstream.** SACC files feed into `validate_sacc` (completeness check) and were intended for `eDR1like` likelihood evaluation. In practice, the eDR1like pipeline reads PKL format (see `edr1like-likelihood-reads-pkl-a6201d1d`), so SACC files are a reference format and exchange medium, while PKL provides the actual likelihood input.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/combine_cross_spectra.py`
fibers.outcome: Upgraded shear SACC tracers from MiscTracer to NZTracer. n(z) loaded from RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits (bins 1-6 + summed 'all' bin). CMB kappa stays MiscTracer. Config: nz.wl path added to config.yaml. Snakefile: nz_file input added to combine_shear_cross_spectra. Script: combine_cross_spectra.py loads n(z) via fitsio, creates NZTracer with z=linspace(0,6,3000), nz from FITS. Both ACT and SPT SACC files rebuilt and validated (14 NZTracer + 1 MiscTracer each, 560 data points).

fibers.body:

`https://shared.euclid.pic.es:8453/tr1/WL/euclid_tr1_le3_v1_1/2D_MASS/`

Same credentials as in `inputs/euclid/download_tr1_systematics.sh` (user: euclid012).

Not worth downloading for current analysis ŌĆö version mismatch with our v2.1 catalog would introduce uncontrolled systematics. fibers.title: Validation: negative cross-spectra vs Margherita positive reference
fibers.body:
All 28 E-mode cross-spectra are systematically negative. Higher-z bins have stronger negative signal (grows with redshift, consistent with a real cosmological signal with wrong sign). Both ACT and SPT, both lensmc and metacal.

## Corrected understanding (iteration 7)

**Amplitude comparison was misleading.** Margherita's reference pickle (`cls_kggg_eRR2_...`) contains *galaxy density ├Ś ╬║* cross-correlations (`kappa_g0`, `g0_g0`, etc.) at ~10^-7 level. Our shear ├Ś ╬║ at ~10^-10 is the expected order of magnitude for cosmic shear. The "30-50├Ś too small" claim was comparing apples to oranges.

**The sign is the real issue.** All 28 spectra have negative E-mode weighted mean:
- Signal grows more negative at higher z: bin1 ~ -4e-9, bin6 ~ -1.6e-8
- High-z bins detected at SNR 4-6 but with wrong sign
- ACT convention confirmed standard (positive ╬║ = overdensity)

## Root cause analysis: (e1, e2) ŌåÆ (Q, U) mapping

Both our code and Margherita's gctools use `[e1, -e2]` for NaMaster NmtField creation. NaMaster docs say this is the correct mapping for IAU convention (e1 along Dec, e2 flipped for sense-of-rotation). But this assumes the catalog stores e1 along *declination* (IAU).

**If Euclid stores e1 along RA** (standard weak lensing convention: e1 > 0 = stretched E-W), then the correct mapping to HEALPix (Q, U) is `[-e1, e2]`:
- Standard WL to IAU is a 90┬░ rotation: Q_IAU = -e1, U_IAU = -e2
- IAU to HEALPix: Q_HP = Q_IAU, U_HP = -U_IAU
- So: [Q_HP, U_HP] = [-e1, e2]
- Our `[e1, -e2]` = `[-Q_HP, -U_HP]` ŌåÆ synthetic test confirms this gives negative E

**Synthetic NaMaster test at nside=64** (correlated ╬║ + shear with known positive C_l^{╬║E}):
- `[+Q, +U]`: E = +6.9e-8 (POSITIVE, correct)
- `[+Q, -U]`: E = +2.2e-9 (weakly positive)
- `[-Q, +U]`: E = -2.2e-9 (negative)
- `[-Q, -U]`: E = -6.9e-8 (NEGATIVE ŌĆö matches our observation)

## Diagnostic in progress

`workflow/scripts/sign_test_lean.py` ŌĆö tests all 4 sign combos on real lensmc bin5 ├Ś ACT data at nside=64. Left running (I/O-limited on cluster filesystem loading the 400MB ACT kappa map). Script reads sparse shear maps efficiently, downgrades immediately to nside=64.

## Open question: what is Euclid's e1/e2 convention?

The DPDD XML schema (`euc-she.xml`) defines `SHE_GENERIC_E1_CORRECTED` as "corrected first component of ellipticity" without specifying the reference direction. Need to check:
1. The LensMC paper (Congedo et al.) for convention definition
2. The SHE-WL pipeline documentation
3. Or just let the `sign_test_lean.py` diagnostic resolve it empirically

## Next steps
1. Wait for `sign_test_lean.py` to complete ŌåÆ tells which combo gives positive E
2. If `[-e1, e2]` wins: fix `make_shear_field()` in `compute_cross_spectrum.py`
3. Rerun one spectrum via Snakemake to verify
4. Note: Margherita also uses `[e1, -e2]` ŌĆö if the convention is wrong, both codes have the same bug (her galaxy ├Ś ╬║ results wouldn't be affected since that's spin-0)
fibers.outcome: Resolved. Sign convention was [e1, -e2] (IAU assumption) but Euclid uses standard WL (e1 along RA). Changed to [-e1, e2]. All 28 E-mode cross-spectra now positive with physically sensible shape and amplitude (~10^-8 to 10^-10). Both LensMC and MetaCal agree. See fiber sign-convention-for-standard-wl-7ebc8d73.

fibers.title: Plot scaling: ŌäōCŌäō instead of Ōäō(Ōäō+1)CŌäō/(2ŽĆ)
fibers.outcome: Changed cross-spectrum plot prefactor from Ōäō(Ōäō+1)/(2ŽĆ) to Ōäō. The Ōäō┬▓-weighted scaling compressed the signal at Ōäō~few hundred where we have the most sensitivity, making plots dominated by high-Ōäō scatter. ŌäōCŌäō gives better visual dynamic range. Updated in workflow/scripts/plot_cross_spectrum.py (line 86). All 28 shear cross-spectrum plots regenerated.

fibers.title: Shear e1/e2 ŌåÆ HEALPix Q/U sign convention
fibers.body:
What is the correct mapping from Euclid catalog (e1, e2) to NaMaster (Q, U)?

## Context

NaMaster expects spin-2 fields in HEALPix convention: Q along growing colatitude (south), U at +45┬░ in direction of growing Žå. Three possible source conventions:

| Convention | e1 reference | To NaMaster |
|---|---|---|
| IAU (e1 along Dec/North) | North | `[e1, -e2]` |
| Standard WL (e1 along RA/East) | East | `[-e1, e2]` |
| HEALPix (e1 along colatitude/South) | South | `[e1, e2]` |

Both our code and Margherita's gctools use `[e1, -e2]`, assuming IAU. But the standard weak lensing convention (DES, HSC, KiDS) defines e1 along RA.

## Derivation (if standard WL convention)

Reference frame rotation from East to South = +ŽĆ/2:
- Q_IAU = e1 cos(2┬ĘŽĆ/2) + e2 sin(2┬ĘŽĆ/2) = -e1
- U_IAU = -e1 sin(2┬ĘŽĆ/2) + e2 cos(2┬ĘŽĆ/2) = -e2
- Q_HP = Q_IAU = -e1, U_HP = -U_IAU = e2
- NaMaster input: `[-e1, e2]`

Our current `[e1, -e2]` = `[-Q_HP, -U_HP]` ŌåÆ explains the negative E-mode.

## Evidence

**Synthetic test:** At nside=64 with known positive C_l^{╬║E}:
- `[Q, U]` ŌåÆ +6.9e-8 (correct)
- `[-Q, -U]` ŌåÆ -6.9e-8 (our current result, negative)

**Real data:** All 28 spectra negative, signal grows with z (real cosmological signal with wrong sign).

**ACT convention:** Confirmed standard (positive ╬║ = overdensity). Not the issue.

## Diagnostic

`workflow/scripts/sign_test_lean.py` ŌĆö runs all 4 combos on real lensmc bin5 ├Ś ACT at nside=64. Left running (slow cluster I/O). Should resolve empirically which combo gives positive E.

**Killed twice by OOM on login node** (loading 400MB ACT kappa map). Needs to run as a SLURM job, or the script needs to be modified to use `dtype=np.float32` and/or read ACT kappa at lower resolution. Alternatively, add as a Snakemake rule with `mem_mb=8000`.

Run it (needs compute node or more memory):
```bash
singularity exec --bind /leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083 \
    /leonardo_work/EUHPC_E05_083/cdaley00/container \
    python workflow/scripts/sign_test_lean.py
```

## What would change

If fix is `[-e1, e2]`:
1. `compute_cross_spectrum.py:make_shear_field()` line 77: `[e1, -e2]` ŌåÆ `[-e1, e2]`
2. `compute_systematic_cross_spectrum.py`: same pattern
3. All 28 cross-spectra and 70 systematics need recomputation
4. Evidence/PTE values will change (may resolve the "suspiciously good PTE" issue too)

Note: Margherita's gctools also uses `[e1, -e2]` for the same shear maps. If this is wrong, both codes have the same bug. (Galaxy density ├Ś ╬║ is spin-0 so unaffected.)

## Alternative: maybe Euclid IS IAU convention

If the SHE pipeline defines e1 along declination (IAU), then `[e1, -e2]` is correct and the negative signal has a different cause. Need to resolve by:
1. Checking SHE/LensMC documentation for the convention definition
2. Or the empirical diagnostic (sign_test_lean.py)
fibers.outcome: Resolved empirically. Applied [-e1, e2] mapping and all cross-spectra turned positive. Euclid e1 is along RA (standard WL convention). The derivation in the body of this fiber was correct: rotation RAŌåÆcolatitude = +ŽĆ/2 gives Q=-e1, U=e2. Note: gctools also uses [e1, -e2] ŌĆö this is a latent bug there too, only visible in spin-2 ├Ś spin-0 cross-spectra (shear auto-spectra are sign-invariant).

fibers.title: Margherita reference is galaxy├Ś╬║ not shear├Ś╬║
fibers.outcome: Margherita's pickle (cls_kggg_eRR2) contains galaxy density ├Ś ╬║ at ~10^-7, not shear ├Ś ╬║. Our ~10^-10 amplitude is the correct order for shear ├Ś ╬║. The amplitude comparison in the validation fiber was apples-to-oranges. Only the sign issue is real.

fibers.title: Sign convention: [-e1, e2] for standard WL
fibers.outcome: Applied [-e1, e2] mapping (standard WL convention: e1 along RA). Euclid follows standard WL (e1 along East/RA), not IAU (e1 along Dec). Frame rotation RAŌåÆcolatitude = +ŽĆ/2 gives Q=-e1, U=e2. Changed compute_cross_spectrum.py and compute_systematic_cross_spectrum.py. gctools [e1, -e2] assumes IAU ŌĆö both codes had the same bug because shear auto-spectra are sign-invariant (only cross-spectra with scalar fields are affected). All 98 spectra recomputing.

fibers.title: CMB mask: apodize instead of square
fibers.outcome: Reversed the earlier 'square' decision. Now following gctools pattern: apodize(mask, 0.15, C2). The mask^2 approach was supported by ACT├ŚDES (Shaikh+2023) but gctools uses apodize for consistency with its iNKA covariance. Switching to match gctools as the reference implementation. Updated make_cmb_mask in compute_cross_spectrum.py.

fibers.title: Covariance: decoupled auto-spectra instead of coupled/fsky
fibers.outcome: Investigated three approaches for systematic covariance: (1) Decoupled auto-spectra ŌĆö FAILED, unbin_cell zeros out modes outside binning range giving even smaller covariance. (2) Knox formula Var=C^aa*C^bb/n_modes ŌĆö WORKS, gives chi2/dof~1.0 for null tests. (3) NaMaster Gaussian overestimates by 10-130x at fsky~0.01 (coupled/fsky approximation breaks down). Knox formula adopted as primary, NaMaster kept for reference. Also found HP UNSEEN bug: systematic maps contained sentinel values that dominated all cross-spectra. Knox chi2/dof now reveals real contamination patterns: galactic extinction is dominant (chi2/dof 1.3-9.0), stellar density moderate, observational systematics clean.

fibers.title: HP UNSEEN values in systematic maps contaminate cross-spectra
fibers.outcome: read_partial_map preserves HP UNSEEN sentinel values (~-1.6375e+30) from sparse FITS. The footprint check (sysmap != 0) includes these pixels, which dominate the cross-spectrum with identical huge values across all systematic maps. Fixed by setting |sysmap| > 1e20 to zero before computing footprint. Effect: pre-fix, all systematic maps produced identical cross-spectra (exposures == SAA == zodiacal for same bin). Post-fix, each map probes its actual correlation with shear.

fibers.title: Galactic extinction contaminates shear cross-spectra
fibers.outcome: Knox null tests reveal galactic extinction ├Ś shear has chi2/dof 1.3-9.0 across bins, PTEŌēł0 for bins 1,4,5,6,all in both lensmc and metacal. Signal increases with bin number (higher-z galaxies behind more extinction). Other systematics (exposures, SAA, zodiacal light) are clean (chi2/dof~1). Stellar density shows moderate contamination (chi2/dof up to 2.8). Galactic extinction correlation needs masking or modeling before unblinded analysis.

fibers.title: Euclid x CMB-kappa: 28/28 cross-spectra detected at 3sigma
fibers.outcome: Knox formula SNR: 28/28 at 3sigma, 27/28 at 5sigma. Median SNR=8.2, max=11.75 (lensmc bin6 x SPT). Both lensmc and metacal consistent. SNR increases with bin number (higher z = more lensing signal). SPT slightly higher SNR than ACT. NaMaster SNR ~50% of Knox (covariance overestimation). Previous iteration: NaMaster showed only 25/28 at 3sigma, 6/28 at 5sigma.

fibers.title: Research tapestry: inspectable cross-correlation analysis
fibers.body:
Build up a research tapestry ŌĆö an inspectable, evolving web of fibers documenting every analysis, comparison, assumption, and result in the Euclid ├Ś CMB-╬║ cross-correlation pipeline. Each fiber is tied to a snakemake rule that produces plots and evidence. The tapestry is the analysis.

## Desired State

### The tapestry

Every snakemake rule that produces a plot or evidence has a **claim fiber** with a `tapestry:{rule_name}` tag. The specName matches the snakemake rule name, linking fiber ŌåÆ `results/claims/{rule_name}/evidence.json` ŌåÆ plots. Each fiber's outcome describes what was found, whether it's sensible, and what assumptions underlie it. Open questions are filed as investigation or question fibers. Decisions about methodology get decision fibers. The DAG of fibers mirrors the DAG of the analysis ŌĆö walking `felt upstream` from any result should explain how we got there.

Evidence + plots co-located in `results/claims/{rule_name}/`. Staleness propagates via evidence.json mtimes. See `/tapestry` skill for rendering details.

Check coverage: `grep -l 'tapestry:' .felt/*.md` vs the list of rules in `workflow/Snakefile` and `workflow/rules/explorations.smk`. Gaps are work.

### Specific analyses

The pipeline should produce and document these comparisons. Each is a snakemake rule (or set of rules) with plots, evidence, and a claim fiber:

- **ACT vs SPT cross-spectra** ŌĆö same shear bins crossed with different CMB experiments. Are they consistent? Differences reveal CMB-side systematics. Plot: side-by-side or ratio, per bin.
- **lensmc vs metacal** ŌĆö same CMB ├Ś different shear methods. Consistency validates shear measurement. Plot: overlay or difference, per bin ├Ś CMB.
- **Redshift trend** ŌĆö C^{E╬║}_Ōäō vs tomographic bin. Signal should increase with bin number (lensing kernel overlap). Plot: all bins on one panel per CMB experiment.
- **B-mode null test** ŌĆö C^{B╬║}_Ōäō should be consistent with noise. Elevated B-modes signal systematics or EŌåÆB leakage (documented in `eb-leakage-inflates-b-mode-800dcbf4` ŌĆö note fsky effects).
- **Systematic contamination tests (expanded)** ŌĆö following DES├ŚSPT (2203.12440, ┬¦A.4, Eq. A.4.1), compute the harmonic-space contamination metric:

  X^f_S(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S}_Ōäō ┬Ę C^{fS}_Ōäō / C^{SS}_Ōäō

  where S is a systematic map, f is the Euclid tracer (shear or density), and ╬║ is CMB convergence. This has the same units as C^{f╬║}_Ōäō and can be compared directly to the signal. It's zero unless the systematic correlates with *both* tracers simultaneously ŌĆö the key insight for cross-correlations.

  The DES├ŚSPT test is done in configuration space (angular correlation functions). The harmonic-space version requires care: C^{SS}_Ōäō in the denominator has noise bias from pixelization and sparse sampling of the systematic maps. This must be understood or debiased ŌĆö noise inflates the denominator and makes contamination look smaller than it is. Options: use the Knox noise estimate for the systematic auto, or subtract an analytical noise floor. Document the choice.

  **What to compute and plot per systematic:**
  - C^{fS}_Ōäō ŌĆö shear ├Ś systematic (currently done)
  - C^{╬║S}_Ōäō ŌĆö CMB ╬║ ├Ś systematic (new ŌĆö add as a snakemake rule)
  - X^f_S(Ōäō) ŌĆö the combined contamination metric
  - Compare X to the statistical uncertainty on C^{f╬║}_Ōäō (DES├ŚSPT uses 1ŌĆō10% of Žā as a reference band)

  **Summary statistic**: Žć┬▓/dof and PTE for X^f_S consistent with zero. DES├ŚSPT uses PTE > 0.01 as the threshold for concern. Galactic extinction is known to contaminate (fiber `galactic-extinction-ecfc9972`); others should be clean.
- **Theory comparison** ŌĆö measured C^{E╬║}_Ōäō overplotted with theoretical prediction from `eDR1like.Theory` (CCL/CAMB). Validates amplitude, shape, and redshift dependence. Requires choosing fiducial cosmology + bias model ŌĆö document choices in decision fibers.
- **Covariance documentation** ŌĆö a fiber (or set of fibers) describing how the covariance is calculated: what approximation (Knox, iNKA, Gaussian), what input spectra, what fsky, what noise model. Every assumption explicit. The Knox formula is primary for systematic null tests; iNKA Gaussian (NaMaster) for signal covariance. Document why, and what the known limitations are.
- **Catalog vs map agreement** ŌĆö already done (fiber `catalog-cross-spectra-validate-c2ced916`), but should have a summary plot rule if it doesn't already.

### Plot quality

Every plot should be publication-ready: clear axis labels (Ōäō, ŌäōC_Ōäō), consistent color scheme (`sns.set_palette("husl", n)`), error bars from the covariance diagonal, legend identifying what's shown. Formatting issues are bugs ŌĆö fix them when found.

### Assumptions register

Methodology choices accumulate in decision fibers. Each should state what was chosen, why, and what alternatives exist. Examples already filed: sign convention, CMB mask treatment, plot scaling, noise model. New ones emerge as the analysis deepens. Check `felt ls --all | grep decision` for the current state.

### Fiber content and connectivity

Claim fibers should be substantive, not just tags with outcomes. Each claim fiber needs:

- **Body text** describing what the analysis does, what it tests, what assumptions it relies on, and what was found. Currently 15/16 tapestry fibers have zero body content ŌĆö they're titles + outcomes only. The body is what makes the tapestry readable by a human walking the graph.
- **Inter-fiber dependencies** matching the Snakemake DAG. Currently every claim fiber depends only on the spec fiber ŌĆö the tapestry view shows isolated nodes with no edges between them. Claim fibers should link to their upstream claims (e.g., `plot_theory_comparison` depends on `compute_theory_cls` and `compute_cross_spectrum`; `plot_contamination_metric` depends on `compute_cmbk_systematic_cross_spectrum` and `compute_systematic_cross_spectrum`). The felt DAG should mirror the analysis DAG.
- **Evidence files** at `results/claims/{rule_name}/evidence.json` with quantitative metrics. The evidence pipeline is being extended to cover all comparison rules (method_comparison, theory_comparison, redshift_trend, contamination_metric, systematic_null_tests, catalog_validation_summary).

### What "done" means

This loop has no stopping criterion ŌĆö the analysis extends over months. Each iteration should make progress: add a fiber, improve a plot, document an assumption, add a comparison, fix a formatting issue, explore something in Margherita's code or the docs. File open questions for the user when taste or judgment is needed.

The tapestry is done when every detail is either addressed or raised. Since research generates new questions faster than it answers them, this is an asymptote, not a destination.

### Authority

The loop has full authority to decide implementation details ŌĆö rule structure, plot design, binning choices, covariance methods, etc. When a decision requires taste or domain judgment, file it as an open child fiber (`-t question`) so the user can weigh in. But make a preliminary choice so progress continues ŌĆö the fiber records the question, the code records the working answer. Both can be revised later.

fibers.title: ACT vs SPT: same galaxies, same spectrum
fibers.body:
The same Euclid shear field crossed with two independent CMB lensing reconstructions ŌĆö ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV ŌĆö should give the same spectrum. If it doesn't, something is wrong on the CMB side.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (lensmc baseline) shows it directly: salmon ACT and teal SPT data points track each other across all bins and multipoles. You can see both the agreement and the noise structure ŌĆö SPT error bars widen at low Ōäō where its smaller sky area limits the mode count, while ACT's larger footprint gives tighter constraints there. At high Ōäō they converge. All 6 bins pass the shape consistency test (median chi2/dof = 0.86, median PTE = 0.63, 0 failures at 5%).

The agreement holds despite ACT and SPT showing different *amplitudes* relative to theory (A_lens = 0.93 vs 0.76). The chi2 test compares shape, not normalization ŌĆö it cannot detect a uniform multiplicative offset. The SPT deficit is exactly that: a uniform ~24% suppression, consistent with a CMB-side calibration factor rather than Ōäō-dependent systematics.

A subtlety beneath the agreement: ACT is contaminated by stellar density and galactic extinction (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`: bias significance up to 8Žā in some bins), while SPT is clean (<0.1Žā after marginalization). This doesn't violate the consistency test because the contamination has the same sign as the signal ŌĆö it inflates ACT, shifting its normalization up, but the shape remains consistent with SPT.

For the method-independence test (lensmc vs metacal), see `tapestry:plot_method_comparison`. For SPT estimator variants, see `tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`.

## Comments
**2026-02-16 00:39** ŌĆö ACT vs SPT comparison plot needs proper xlims ŌĆö current axis range doesn't show the data well.
fibers.outcome: ACT and SPT cross-spectra are fully consistent: 14/14 pass at 5% level, median PTE=0.639, median chi2/dof=0.86. No evidence of CMB-side systematics. Evidence + plots co-located at results/claims/plot_act_vs_spt/.

fibers.title: Redshift trend: signal rises with lensing kernel overlap
fibers.body:
All 6 tomographic bins on a single panel, testing the basic lensing prediction: the cross-correlation amplitude should increase with source redshift, because the CMB ╬║ lensing kernel (peaking at z ~ 2) has more overlap with higher-redshift galaxies.

**It does.** S/N ranges from ~5Žā (bin 1ŌĆō2, low lensing kernel overlap) to ~12Žā (bin 6, maximum overlap). The ordering matches ╬øCDM predictions. Theory overlays (Planck 2018 with standard NLA IA) track the measured redshift dependence.

**Low-z complication.** Bins 1ŌĆō2 are where two effects compete. Systematic contamination from stellar density *boosts* the ACT signal (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`), while intrinsic alignments *suppress* the theory prediction (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). In raw data, these partially cancel ŌĆö the fit looks good, but for the wrong reasons. After template marginalization (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`), the low-z ACT amplitude drops. The redshift trend with raw data is diagnostic, not definitive, for the low bins.

**High-z clean.** Bins 4ŌĆō6 are clean on both sides: contamination is negligible, IA suppression is small (<10%), and the ACT data matches theory well. These bins carry the bulk of the cosmological constraining power.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_redshift_trend.py`
fibers.outcome: Signal increases with redshift bin as expected from lensing kernel overlap. S/N 5-12, highest in bin 6. Low-z bins (1-2) complicated by contamination + IA degeneracy.

fibers.title: lensmc vs metacal: pipeline independence
fibers.body:
Two independent shear measurement pipelines ŌĆö lensmc (forward-model PSF fitting) and metacal (metacalibration with sheared images) ŌĆö process the same galaxies in different ways. If their cross-spectra with CMB ╬║ agree, neither introduces a measurement-specific bias.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (ACT baseline) makes the agreement visible: blue lensmc and orange metacal data points sit on top of each other in every bin, with fractional differences scattering around zero in the sub-panels. The residuals are mostly within ┬▒10% even at noisy bandpowers. Median chi2/dof = 0.14 ŌĆö suspiciously tight, but expected. The two methods share the *same galaxies* and the *same CMB map*, so their noise is highly correlated. The Knox formula assumes independent noise realizations; applied to the difference of correlated measurements it dramatically overestimates the variance, deflating chi2. This test confirms consistency but has limited discriminating power. A proper test would need a covariance for the difference spectrum accounting for shared noise.

Despite producing consistent cross-spectra, the two methods couple differently to systematic contamination. After template marginalization, lensmc retains 75% of its raw S/N (20Žā ŌåÆ 15Žā) while metacal retains 69% (19Žā ŌåÆ 13Žā). The different shear weight maps couple differently to spatially-varying systematics. Both methods give consistent final results, but lensmc is preferable for contamination-sensitive analyses.
fibers.outcome: 35 comparisons (7 bins x 5 CMB experiments) all pass. Median chi2/dof=0.14, PTE=1.00 ŌĆö low chi2 expected from correlated noise (same galaxies, same CMB map). No evidence of shear measurement method bias. metacal ~2x more sensitive to systematic contamination than lensmc (different weight coupling).

fibers.title: CMB kappa x systematic null tests: galactic extinction contaminates both sides
fibers.body:
The CMB-side leg of the contamination diagnostic: cross-spectra between CMB ╬║ maps and systematic templates. Required to compute the full DES├ŚSPT contamination metric X^f_S(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S}_Ōäō ┬Ę C^{fS}_Ōäō / C^{SS}_Ōäō.

**What it tests.** Cross-correlations are immune to systematics that contaminate only one tracer ŌĆö the key advantage over auto-spectra. The contamination metric is non-zero only if a systematic correlates with *both* ╬║ and the Euclid tracer simultaneously. C^{╬║S} measures whether the systematic leaks into the CMB lensing reconstruction (e.g. dust biasing the CMB gradient estimator).

**Results.** 25 spectra (5 sysmaps ├Ś 5 CMB experiments). Galactic extinction strongly contaminates all CMB ╬║ reconstructions: ACT (chi2=68.6/20, PTE=0), SPT GMV (chi2=65.3/20, PTE=0), SPT TT (chi2=48.9/20, PTE=0.0003), SPT PP and MV similar. All other systematics (stellar density, exposures, SAA, zodiacal) are clean across all estimators. This parallels DES├ŚSPT findings (2203.12440) where dust was the dominant contaminant.

Importantly, galactic extinction contamination is **CMB-estimator-independent** ŌĆö all 4 SPT estimators (GMV, TT, PP, MV) plus ACT show comparable contamination. This confirms the dust signal is in the CMB sky, not an estimator artifact.

**C^{SS} resolved.** The full X metric now uses C^{SS} from systematic auto-spectra (stored as `cl_sys_auto` in NPZ files). See fiber `contamination-metric-needs-c-ss-6e451610`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/compute_cmbk_systematic_cross_spectrum.py`
fibers.outcome: 25 cross-spectra (5 sysmaps x 5 CMB). Galactic extinction contaminates all CMB ╬║ reconstructions: ACT chi2=68.6/20, SPT GMV chi2=65.3/20, SPT TT/PP/MV similar. 4/25 fail at 5%. Median chi2/dof=0.96 overall. All non-extinction systematics clean. Confirms dust contamination is CMB-estimator-independent.

fibers.title: Covariance methodology
fibers.body:
Two covariance estimators used in the pipeline, with different regimes of validity.

## Knox formula (primary for null tests)

`Var(C^{ab}_b) = C^{aa}_b ┬Ę C^{bb}_b / n_modes_b`

where `n_modes_b = ╬Ż_ŌäōŌłłb (2Ōäō+1) ┬Ę fsky_eff` and `fsky_eff = Ō¤©wŌéü┬ĘwŌééŌ¤®┬▓ / Ō¤©wŌéü┬▓┬ĘwŌéé┬▓Ō¤®` (Hivon et al. 2002). This is diagonal ŌĆö no bandpower-to-bandpower correlations.

**Inputs.** C^{aa} and C^{bb} are the auto-spectra of the two fields. For shear ├Ś CMB ╬║: C^{╬│╬│} includes shape noise (`NOISE_CL` from FITS headers), C^{╬║╬║} includes reconstruction noise. fsky_eff computed from the apodized product of both masks.

**When it works.** Accurate at all fsky when the Gaussian approximation holds (random Gaussian fields, no survey geometry effects). Primary for systematic null tests where the signal is zero by construction ŌĆö the Knox formula with actual auto-spectra gives properly calibrated chi2/dof ~ 1 under H0.

**Known limitation.** Missing off-diagonal terms (bandpower-to-bandpower coupling from the mask), super-sample variance. These matter for parameter inference but not for null tests.

## NaMaster Gaussian (iNKA)

NaMaster's `gaussian_covariance()` computes the full Gaussian covariance including mode-coupling from the survey mask. In principle more accurate than Knox for structured masks.

**The problem at fsky ~ 0.01.** NaMaster overestimates variance by 10-130├Ś for our individual tomographic bins. The cause is documented in fibers `suspiciously-good-ptes-in-dbf44cbb` and `systematic-pte-overestimation-89f57308`: at very small sky fractions, the iNKA approximation breaks down or the mode-coupling kernel overwhelms the diagonal terms. This manifests as all PTEs being ~ 1.0 (variance so large that any signal is within "noise").

**Use case.** Retained as a secondary diagnostic and for SACC file packaging. Not used for chi2/PTE in evidence JSON ŌĆö Knox is authoritative there.

## Choice rationale

Knox is the right tool for the current analysis stage (signal detection, null tests). For parameter inference with the full eDR1like likelihood, a proper covariance (potentially from simulations or the analytic Gaussian with correct coupling) would be needed. This is downstream work, not blocking current validation.
fibers.outcome: Knox analytic (primary for null tests): Var=C^aa*C^bb/n_modes with fsky_eff from Hivon 2002. NaMaster Gaussian (iNKA): overestimates 10-130x at fsky~0.01. Knox authoritative for chi2/PTE. See fibers systematic-pte-overestimation-89f57308, suspiciously-good-ptes-in-dbf44cbb for details.

fibers.title: Theory comparison: Planck 2018 predicts the ACT signal
fibers.body:
The hero figure (`tapestry:plot_signal_overview`) shows data against vanilla Planck ╬øCDM. Here we add the full theory: ╬øCDM + NLA intrinsic alignment (A_IA=1.72, ╬Ę=-0.41 from Euclid pipeline defaults ŌĆö see `nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). Both curves are predictions, not fits: cosmology from Planck, IA from Euclid, binned through NaMaster bandpower windows.

The plot shows two theory lines per bin: vanilla Planck 2018 (black) and NLA IA (crimson dashed). The IA correction suppresses the prediction by 4-36% depending on redshift ŌĆö largest at low-z where the GI term geometry is most favorable.

**ACT is consistent with standard theory.** Joint chi2 = 106/120 (PTE 0.82) against the NLA prediction. The vanilla prediction slightly overshoots ŌĆö adding standard IA brings it into line. This isn't a fit; it's a prediction matching data.

**SPT shows a 24% deficit.** Joint chi2 = 226/120 (PTE Ōēł 0). Data sits below both theory curves. The deficit is method-independent (lensmc Ōēł metacal), scale-dependent (worse at high Ōäō ŌĆö see `tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`), and absent from ACT. This points to a CMB-side normalization issue, characterized further in `tapestry:plot_theory_residual_analysis`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_theory_comparison.py`
fibers.outcome: ACT consistent with Planck 2018 ╬øCDM + standard NLA IA (PTE 0.82). SPT shows 24% normalization deficit (PTEŌēł0), method-independent, CMB-side. Shown: lensmc ├Ś {ACT, SPT GMV} ŌĆö metacal and SPT variants covered by method_comparison and spt_estimator nodes.

fibers.title: Contamination metric: small per bandpower, dangerous in aggregate
fibers.body:
A systematic can only contaminate a cross-correlation if it correlates with *both* tracers simultaneously. The contamination metric X^f_S(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S} ┬Ę C^{fS} / C^{SS}, following DES├ŚSPT (2203.12440 ┬¦A.4), quantifies exactly this: it has the same units as the signal C^{f╬║} and can be compared directly to the measurement uncertainty.

The composite figure shows X(Ōäō) for all five systematics (stellar density, galactic extinction, exposures, zodiacal light, SAA) crossed with the two baseline CMB reconstructions (ACT and SPT-GMV). Six redshift bins are overlaid in each panel; gray bands mark 1% and 10% of the signal Knox uncertainty. Per bandpower, all contamination metrics sit within or below the 1% band ŌĆö small enough to look safe. Even galactic extinction, which individually contaminates both ╬║ (chi2 ~ 49ŌĆō69/20) and shear (chi2/dof up to 9 in the null tests), produces sub-percent per-bandpower bias. This is the fundamental advantage of cross-correlations: contamination requires a triple coincidence.

But per-bandpower smallness is misleading. Twenty bandpowers each contributing <1% can integrate to a multi-Žā bias. The C^{SS} denominator carries noise bias from downgrading sparse systematic maps (nside 16384ŌåÆ2048), inflating the denominator and making X a *lower bound*. The bias budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) integrates over multipoles and reveals that stellar density reaches 4.4Žā and galactic extinction 3.7Žā for ACT ŌĆö individually small, collectively dangerous. Where the budget flags significant bias, template marginalization handles it (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`).
fibers.outcome: Per-bandpower contamination metric X = C^{╬║S}┬ĘC^{fS}/C^{SS} sits below 1% of signal uncertainty for all five systematics across all CMB estimators. However, the integrated bias significance (tapestry:plot_systematic_budget) reaches multi-Žā levels for stellar density and galactic extinction ŌĆö per-bandpower smallness does not guarantee negligible integrated bias.

fibers.title: Individual cross-spectrum plots: 28 positive spectra visualized
fibers.body:
Visual inspection of every individual cross-spectrum. The first-order check before any comparison or combination: does each spectrum look physical?

**Layout.** Two-panel figure per spectrum. Top: E-mode (signal) with Knox error bars, Ōäō┬ĘC_Ōäō scaling. Bottom: B-mode (null check) ŌĆö should be consistent with zero for a real lensing signal. Annotation box shows SNR and PTE.

**B-mode caveat.** At the partial sky coverage of individual tomographic bins (fsky ~ 0.01), NaMaster EŌåÆB leakage can produce B-mode amplitudes up to ~93% of E-mode. The B-mode variance is ~500├Ś smaller than E-mode (since C_BB << C_EE), so elevated B-mode chi2 is expected from leakage, not from systematics. B-mode is diagnostic, not a reliable null test at this fsky.

**28 spectra.** All show positive E-mode signal. S/N ranges from ~3 (bin1, small lensing kernel overlap) to ~12 (bin6, maximum overlap). The visual impression is the starting point for every downstream comparison ŌĆö method differences, CMB experiment consistency, and redshift trends all build on these individual measurements.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_cross_spectrum.py`
fibers.outcome: 28 plots (2 methods x 7 bins x 2 CMB). Two-panel layout: E-mode signal (top), B-mode null check (bottom). ell*Cl scaling. Knox error bars when available. SNR/PTE annotation. All spectra show positive E-mode signal.

fibers.title: Systematic null tests: galactic extinction correlates with shear
fibers.body:
Do observational systematics correlate with the shear signal? This is the first-pass diagnostic ŌĆö cross-correlate each systematic map with the Euclid shear field and test whether the result is consistent with zero.

The 2├Ś3 panel shows per-bandpower pulls (C^{fS}_Ōäō / Žā_Knox) for all five systematics across six tomographic bins. Under the null hypothesis, pulls scatter around zero with unit variance ŌĆö the ┬▒1Žā and ┬▒2Žā gray bands mark the expected range. Coherent excursions beyond ┬▒2Žā signal contamination; their multipole structure shows *where* in harmonic space the systematic leaks in.

**Galactic extinction stands out.** In bins 5 and 6 (highest redshift), the pink pulls reach ┬▒3-5Žā across a broad range of multipoles ŌĆö this is broadband contamination, not a feature at a specific angular scale. The chi2/dof climbs to 7.9 in bin 6. Lower bins show milder excursions (chi2/dof 1.5-2.3). The physical mechanism: Galactic dust affects PSF modeling and photometric calibration, imprinting a large-scale pattern on the shear maps.

**Other systematics are clean.** Stellar density, exposures, SAA, and zodiacal light all scatter within the ┬▒1Žā band in most bins. Stellar density shows occasional 2Žā excursions but no coherent spectral pattern ŌĆö its null test looks benign. This is misleading: the bias budget analysis (`plot_systematic_budget`) reveals stellar density is actually the *dominant* ACT contaminant, because its correlation with CMB ╬║ is stronger than galactic extinction's. The null test only measures C^{fS} (shear ├Ś systematic); the full contamination diagnostic requires C^{╬║S} as well.

**What this test cannot see.** A systematic that correlates with CMB ╬║ but not with shear won't appear here. The contamination metric (`tapestry:plot_contamination_metric`) captures the joint effect ŌĆö and reveals that even galactic extinction's alarming null test failures translate to sub-percent per-bandpower bias, because the triple coincidence required for cross-correlation contamination is much rarer than pairwise correlations suggest. But sub-percent per bandpower can integrate to multi-Žā bias over 20 bandpowers ŌĆö the integrated budget (`tapestry:plot_systematic_budget`) and template marginalization (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`) complete the picture.
fibers.outcome: Spectral pull plot: 5 systematics ├Ś 6 bins showing C^{fS}/Žā_Knox per bandpower. Galactic extinction shows coherent ┬▒3-5Žā pulls at low Ōäō in bins 5-6 (chi2/dof up to 7.9). Clean systematics scatter within ┬▒1Žā. 7/30 lensmc tests fail at 5%. The spectral structure is now visible ŌĆö contamination is broadband, not confined to specific multipoles.

fibers.title: SACC file validation: tracers, n(z), covariance complete
fibers.body:
Automated validation of the combined SACC file, run after `combine_shear_cross_spectra` packages 28 individual NPZ files into a single data product. Catches packaging errors before the SACC file is used downstream ŌĆö missing tracers, unnormalized n(z), dimension mismatches between data vector and covariance.

**What it checks.** (1) All 15 tracers defined: 14 NZTracer (7 bins ├Ś 2 methods, each with z and n(z) arrays) + 1 MiscTracer (CMB ╬║). (2) n(z) physically sensible: non-negative, integrates to ~1 for each bin. (3) Data vector complete: 560 data points (14 spectra ├Ś 40 bandpowers ŌĆö 20 E-mode + 20 B-mode). (4) Covariance matrix shape (560 ├Ś 560) matches data vector. (5) Covariance positive semi-definite (eigenvalue check). Run independently per CMB experiment (ACT, SPT).

**Why this matters.** Dimension mismatches between the data vector and covariance cause silent errors in likelihood evaluation ŌĆö wrong chi2 values with no error message. The tracer check ensures n(z) curves are properly associated with each cross-spectrum, which is critical for theory prediction. A missing or swapped n(z) would bias the theory C_Ōäō comparison.

**Evidence.** Validation results stored at `results/claims/validate_sacc/evidence.json` with pass/fail per check.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/validate_sacc.py`
fibers.outcome: Validates SACC file completeness: tracer definitions, n(z) curves, data vectors, covariance blocks. Run per CMB experiment. SACC files contain NZTracer for shear bins, MiscTracer for CMB kappa.

fibers.title: Weight masking: apodize(binary) * weight following gctools
fibers.outcome: Shear mask = apodize(weight > 0, 0.15, C2) * weight. Binary threshold at weight > 0, C2 apodization at 0.15 rad, then multiply by the weight map. This follows gctools spectra.py::map2wlcls. The apodization smooths the mask boundary to reduce mode coupling artefacts. CMB mask = apodize(cmb_mask, 0.15, C2) directly. Systematic maps use apodize(common_footprint, 0.15, C2) over the intersection of shear+CMB coverage. All compute scripts use the same pattern.

fibers.title: Catalog validation: pixelization introduces no bias
fibers.body:
NmtFieldCatalog works directly from galaxy catalogs, bypassing pixelization. NmtField works from maps built by binning galaxies into HEALPix pixels. If the two give the same cross-spectrum, map construction introduces no bias. Same galaxies, same CMB ╬║, different computational paths.

The 6-panel spectral overlay (lensmc ├Ś ACT) shows them side by side: blue map-based with error bars, orange catalog-based without. They track each other across all bins and multipoles ŌĆö Pearson correlation 0.995-0.999, with the closest agreement at high Ōäō where signal dominates. The RMS fractional differences (7-33%, S/N-weighted) reflect noise scatter in low-S/N bandpowers, not systematic bias. No trend with redshift bin.

This validates the map-based pipeline as the production measurement. The catalog approach serves as an independent check, not a competing estimator ŌĆö it's computationally heavier (64GB for ~5M galaxies at lmax=3000) and provides no covariance estimate.
fibers.outcome: Map-based and catalog-based cross-spectra agree: median correlation 0.998, RMS 7-33% (S/N-weighted). Map pipeline validated ŌĆö no pixelization bias.

fibers.title: Evidence pipelines for all comparison rules
fibers.outcome: Added evidence JSON output to 5 comparison scripts (method_comparison, theory_comparison, redshift_trend, contamination_metric, systematic_null_tests, catalog_validation_summary). Each writes per-wildcard evidence with chi2/PTE/RMS metrics. Snakemake rules updated to output to results/claims/{rule_name}/. Four new aggregation rules added (aggregate_{method,theory,redshift,contamination}_evidence). Variable name collision (evidence_path reused in loops) found and fixed. 7/9 evidence directories now populated; systematic_null_tests and contamination_metric waiting on SLURM reruns of compute_systematic (code changed to add cl_sys_auto).

fibers.title: Theory C_Ōäō computation: eDR1like with Planck 2018 fiducial
fibers.body:
Generates fiducial theory C^{E╬║}_Ōäō predictions for each tomographic bin using `eDR1like.Theory` backed by CCL (Core Cosmology Library) with CAMB as the Boltzmann solver.

**Cosmology.** Planck 2018 best-fit (TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing): A_s=2.105├Ś10^{-9}, n_s=0.9665, H_0=67.66, ╬®_b=0.04897, ╬®_c=0.2607. Galaxy bias b=1 (constant), no magnification.

**Two theory models computed per bin:**

1. **Vanilla** (lensing only) ŌĆö galaxy-galaxy lensing with no intrinsic alignment. This provides the baseline for comparing data amplitude and shape. When A_hat < 1, the deficit flags missing physics (IA, calibration) or CMB-side normalization issues.

2. **NLA intrinsic alignment** ŌĆö nonlinear alignment model with A_IA=1.72, eta_IA=-0.41 (eDR1like defaults, NLA-z parameterization). CCL handles the physics internally: `WeakLensingTracer` with `use_A_ia=True` computes C_1 * rho_cr * Omega_m / D(z) * A_IA * (1+z)^eta_IA. IA suppresses the cross-spectrum at low redshift (36% in bin 1, 4% in bin 6), resolving the ACT amplitude deficit from 3Žā to 1.3Žā. See fiber `nla-intrinsic-alignment-cb1dea0b`.

**Binning.** Unbinned theory C_Ōäō computed at every multipole, then binned through NaMaster's bandpower window functions (Bbl) to match the data resolution. This ensures fair comparison ŌĆö binned theory accounts for the mode-coupling matrix and bandpower averaging. Bbl was already stored in the cross-spectrum NPZ files (`bpws` key, shape (2,20,2,3001)); E-mode extraction: `bpws[0,:,0,:]`.

**n(z).** Source redshift distributions from `RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits` (6 bins, 3000 z-points on linspace(0,6), sum-normalized). Method-specific (lensmc vs metacal have different selection functions).

**Output.** NPZ with keys: `cl_theory_bin{1-6}` (vanilla unbinned), `cl_binned_bin{1-6}` (vanilla binned), `cl_ia_theory_bin{1-6}` (IA unbinned), `cl_ia_binned_bin{1-6}` (IA binned), plus `ell_theory` and metadata. Downstream consumers: theory comparison, residual analysis, likelihood evaluation, signal overview, deprojected likelihood.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/compute_theory_cls.py`
fibers.outcome: Computes C^{E╬║}_Ōäō theory predictions using eDR1like.Theory (CCL/CAMB) at Planck 2018 best-fit. Two models: (1) vanilla lensing-only, (2) NLA IA (A_IA=1.72, eta=-0.41). Both binned through NaMaster bandpower windows. 4 NPZ outputs: 2 methods ├Ś 2 CMB experiments, each containing vanilla and IA predictions for all 6 bins.

fibers.title: Catalog vs map comparison: 28 overlay plots
fibers.body:
Per-wildcard overlay of catalog-based and map-based cross-spectra for visual inspection. The complement to the summary heatmap in `plot_catalog_validation_summary` ŌĆö here you see the full Ōäō-dependence, not just summary statistics.

**Why this matters.** The map-based pipeline converts catalogs to pixelized maps (nside 2048) before computing spectra via NaMaster. This introduces potential biases: sub-pixel averaging, weight map artifacts, and pixelization window function effects. The catalog-based pipeline (`NmtFieldCatalog`) bypasses all of this ŌĆö it works directly from galaxy positions and ellipticities. Agreement between the two confirms that map-making is not introducing artifacts. This is especially important for the low-number-density high-z bins where pixel occupancy is sparse.

**Layout.** Single panel per comparison. Both spectra overlaid with shared x-axis (Ōäō) and Ōäō┬ĘC_Ōäō scaling. Error bars from map-based Knox covariance (no covariance available for catalog-based, since NaMaster doesn't support covariance for NmtFieldCatalog). The visual comparison is the primary diagnostic ŌĆö differences should be invisible compared to error bars.

**28 comparisons.** Differences are sub-percent in well-measured bandpowers. Any visible discrepancy at high Ōäō is consistent with noise ŌĆö catalog-based and map-based spectra use different internal representations of the same underlying field. S/N-weighted RMS fractional differences range 3-46% (SNR weighting from `plot_catalog_validation_summary` downweights noise-dominated high-Ōäō bins).

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_catalog_vs_map_comparison.py`
fibers.outcome: 28 overlay plots (2 methods ├Ś 7 bins ├Ś 2 CMB) comparing catalog-based (NmtFieldCatalog) and map-based (NmtField) cross-spectra. Visual confirmation that map-making doesn't introduce bias. Each panel shows both spectra with shared error bars.

fibers.title: SPT estimators: no foreground bias
fibers.body:
SPT-3G provides four CMB lensing estimator variants with genuinely different foreground sensitivity. GMV (baseline) uses all quadratic pairs ŌĆö highest SNR but most foreground-exposed. TT (temperature-only) is maximally sensitive to tSZ from galaxy clusters. PP (polarization-only) is the cleanest but noisiest. MV (SQE) tests pipeline-level consistency. All share the same survey mask, so differences in their cross-spectra with Euclid shear isolate foreground contamination in the lensing reconstruction.

The 6-panel ratio plot (lensmc baseline) shows each variant divided by GMV as a function of multipole. The ratios scatter around unity in every bin ŌĆö TT (salmon), PP (teal), MV (purple) all consistent with the 10% shaded band, with noise increasing at low Ōäō where each variant's individual S/N is lowest. PP shows the widest scatter (noisiest estimator), but no systematic offset. TT ŌĆö the critical test for tSZ bias ŌĆö tracks GMV tightly. The large error bars at individual bandpowers correctly show that this is a noisy test: the power comes from the aggregate (18 comparisons, median chi2/dof = 0.19), not any single Ōäō bin.

This means tSZ contamination of the SPT lensing reconstruction does not bias the shear ├Ś ╬║ cross-correlation at current noise levels. The SPT normalization deficit (A_lens = 0.76) cannot be explained by foreground leakage ŌĆö it persists identically across all four estimators, including the foreground-immune PP channel. This disfavors foreground-related hypotheses for the deficit (see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`).

## Comments
**2026-02-15 23:56** ŌĆö Plot rework: drop the ratio plot (noise/noise ratios scatter too much at low Ōäō). Instead plot differences relative to best-fit Planck amplitude: (C_variant - C_GMV) / C_theory(A_lens). Clean residual in interpretable units.
fibers.outcome: All 14 comparisons (2 methods x 7 bins) show complete consistency across all 4 SPT estimator variants. Chi2/dof: MV~0.03, TT~0.2, PP~0.3 (all vs GMV, dof=20). Min PTE=0.981 (metacal bin3 PP). No foreground contamination detected ŌĆö tSZ bias in TT estimator does not affect shear x kappa cross-spectra at current noise levels. 42 cross-spectra computed, 14 comparison plots + evidence at results/claims/plot_spt_estimator_comparison/.

fibers.title: Jackknife: galaxy-side systematics are clean
fibers.body:
Multiply each galaxy's ellipticity by a random sign (┬▒1), destroying the coherent cosmological signal while preserving noise structure, pixel coverage, and weight maps. Cross-correlating these randomized shear maps with CMB ╬║ should yield spectra consistent with zero. Any excess reveals galaxy-side additive systematics ŌĆö PSF leakage, selection effects, calibration errors correlated with sky position.

All 24 null spectra (2 methods ├Ś 6 bins ├Ś 2 CMB experiments, 20 bandpowers each) are consistent with zero: median chi2/dof = 0.88, median PTE = 0.62, zero failures at 5%. The galaxy side is clean.

This test is sensitive to additive systematics but blind to multiplicative biases (shear calibration), which are sign-invariant. A single random realization (seed=42) suffices to rule out large additive contamination. For the CMB-side null test (uncorrelated simulations), see `tapestry:plot_sim_null_test`. For spatial structure, see `tapestry:plot_spatial_variation`.
fibers.outcome: 24/24 null cross-spectra (2 methods x 6 bins x 2 CMB) consistent with zero. Median Knox chi2/dof=0.88, median PTE=0.617, 0 failures at 5% threshold. No galaxy-side systematics detected.

fibers.title: SPT theory comparison fails while ACT passes: Knox covariance or calibration?
fibers.body:
## Observation

All SPT ├Ś shear theory comparisons fail at 5% significance (total PTE Ōēł 0), while ACT ├Ś shear theory comparisons pass (PTE = 0.63 for lensmc, 0.45 for metacal). Yet ACT and SPT cross-spectra agree with each other: 14/14 consistent at 5%, median PTE = 0.64.

**Evidence:**
- lensmc ├Ś ACT: total chi2/dof = 114.1/120 = 0.95, PTE = 0.63
- lensmc ├Ś SPT-GMV: total chi2/dof = 249.0/120 = 2.08, PTE Ōēł 0
- Per-bin SPT chi2 inflated across all bins (37.5, 56.0, 42.5, 30.3, 33.5, 49.2 for dof=20)
- Also fails for SPT TT/PP/MV variants ŌĆö not estimator-specific

## Hypotheses

1. **Knox error bars too tight for SPT.** The Knox formula uses `Var = |C^{EE}| ├Ś |C^{╬║╬║}| / n_modes`. If SPT has lower reconstruction noise (smaller `C^{╬║╬║}_auto`), the Knox error bars shrink, inflating chi2 for the same theory-data residuals. Need to compare `C^{╬║╬║}_auto` for ACT vs SPT.

2. **fsky_cross difference.** SPT winter field has different sky overlap with Euclid than ACT DR6. Smaller overlap ŌåÆ fewer modes ŌåÆ larger Knox variance (which would REDUCE chi2). This goes the wrong direction ŌĆö so noise properties dominate if this hypothesis is wrong.

3. **SPT lensing calibration offset.** The SPT maps may have a different lensing response normalization. A multiplicative bias would shift all cross-spectra systematically relative to theory while leaving ACT-SPT ratios consistent (if both are miscalibrated similarly). But ACT-SPT agreement at the difference level argues against this.

4. **Binning mismatch.** The theory curves are computed and binned identically for both CMB experiments. No mechanism for binning to differ.

## Findings

**Knox variance ratio (lensmc bin4):** SPT/ACT = 0.70 (median across bandpowers). SPT error bars are ~84% the size of ACT's. This explains part of the chi2 inflation: for identical residuals, SPT chi2 would be ~1.42├Ś ACT's. Actual ratio is ~2.2├Ś, so the residuals also differ (noise realization + possible systematic).

**Interpretation:** SPT has tighter statistical constraints on the cross-spectrum. A vanilla Planck 2018 theory (no IA, no magnification bias, no multiplicative shear calibration) is "good enough" for ACT's noise level but fails when tested against SPT's tighter constraints. This may not indicate a problem with SPT ŌĆö rather, it reveals that the theory model is insufficient at SPT's statistical power. The ACT comparison passes because it has less power to detect the discrepancy.

## Amplitude fit analysis (iteration 10)

Implemented `plot_theory_residual_analysis` rule. Fits A_hat per bin├Ścmbk. See fiber `theory-residual-analysis-6a66a995` for full results.

**Key finding: it's both calibration AND shape.**

- ACT mean A = 0.83 ┬▒ 0.05 (3.3Žā low); shape chi2 good after fit (PTE 0.73-0.83)
- SPT mean A = 0.70 ┬▒ 0.05 (6.5Žā low); shape chi2 still fails (PTE < 0.001)
- Method-independent: lensmc Ōēł metacal for both CMB experiments

The ~17% common deficit (A < 1 for both ACT and SPT) is consistent with missing IA in the theory. The additional ~15% SPT deficit is CMB-side ŌĆö possibilities: (1) lensing response normalization, (2) noise debiasing, (3) effective Ōäō weighting from noise/mask differences.

SPT shape failure concentrated in low-z bins (1-3), improving at high-z (5-6). Consistent with scale-dependent modeling deficiency (IA, baryonic feedback) that ACT's larger errors can't detect.

## Resolution

All hypotheses have been pursued through subsequent analysis:

- ~~Check if SPT residuals are systematically biased vs scattered~~ ŌåÆ **Done** (iteration 10). Both: multiplicative offset + shape failure.
- ~~Add IA to theory and see if SPT chi2 improves~~ ŌåÆ **Done** (iteration 13). NLA IA (A_IA=1.72, eta=-0.41) resolves ACT (A=0.93, 1.3Žā from 1). SPT improves 0.69ŌåÆ0.76 but 4.9Žā deficit persists. See `nla-intrinsic-alignment-cb1dea0b`.
- ~~Template marginalization~~ ŌåÆ **Done** (iteration 23). Systematic contamination is negligible for SPT (<0.1Žā shift). Deficit is intrinsic. See `template-deprojection-a1621797`.
- ~~Per-bin structure~~ ŌåÆ **Done** (iteration 27). Bins 1ŌĆō3 fail shape (chi2_shape/dof=1.7ŌĆō2.7), bins 4ŌĆō6 pass shape (1.2ŌĆō1.6). Low-z shape failure consistent with SPT lmin=500 filtering. See `spt-deficit-bin-level-structure-de2540dd`.
- Check SPT lensing response normalization documentation ŌåÆ **Not pursued** (requires SPT team).
- Compare ACT vs SPT C^{╬║╬║}_auto ŌåÆ **Partially done**: Knox variance ratio SPT/ACT=0.70 confirmed.

**Conclusion:** The original observation decomposed into (1) common IA suppression (~10%, resolved by NLA IA) + (2) SPT-specific normalization (~24%, CMB-side, not addressable from Euclid side). Low-z shape failure points to SPT reconstruction filtering at lmin=500. Investigation is as complete as possible without SPT team input.
fibers.outcome: Decomposed into two effects: (1) common IA suppression (~10%), fully explained by NLA IA (A_IA=1.72, eta=-0.41) ŌĆö ACT resolved to A=0.93 (1.3Žā from unity); (2) SPT-specific normalization deficit (~24%), method-independent, contamination-independent, with low-z shape failure from lmin=500 filtering. Not addressable from Euclid side. See downstream fibers: nla-intrinsic-alignment, spt-normalization-deficit, spt-deficit-bin-level-structure.

fibers.title: Theory residuals: ACT passes, SPT fails in shape
fibers.body:
The theory comparison (`tapestry:plot_theory_comparison`) shows ACT matching and SPT low. But how much of the SPT deficit is a simple amplitude offset vs. a shape disagreement? This node fits a single lensing amplitude A_lens per bin and tests whether the residual shape ŌĆö data/(├é┬Ętheory) ŌĆö is consistent with noise.

Theory here is NLA IA (see `nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`), the same prediction shown in the theory comparison. Amplitude fits use Knox covariance (diagonal).

| CMB | A_lens | Žā from 1 | shape PTE |
|-----|-------|----------|-----------|
| ACT lensmc | 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 | 1.3Žā | 0.83 |
| SPT-GMV lensmc | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 | 4.9Žā | ~0 |

**ACT passes both tests.** A_lens = 0.93 ŌĆö within 1.3Žā of unity. Shape is excellent. The ~7% residual is consistent with known missing model ingredients (`theory-model-completeness-for-526d66a5`): shear calibration ~2%, baryonic feedback ~3-5%, n(z) shifts ~1-3%.

**SPT fails in both amplitude and shape.** The deficit has bin-level structure: low-z bins (1-3) fail the shape test (chi2/dof 1.7-2.7) while high-z bins (4-6) pass (chi2/dof 1.2-1.6). The Ōäō-range robustness test (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`) reveals scale dependence: high-Ōäō bandpowers actively suppress the amplitude, disfavoring a uniform calibration offset. This is characterized further in `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.
fibers.outcome: Per-bin amplitude fits: ACT A_lens=0.93┬▒0.06 (1.3Žā from unity, shape PTE 0.83), SPT A_lens=0.76┬▒0.05 (4.9Žā, shape fails). ACT consistent with ╬øCDM; SPT deficit is scale-dependent and method-independent.

fibers.title: Analysis summary: one-page synthesis
fibers.body:
Everything in one figure. Four panels compress the full analysis ŌĆö detection, consistency, null tests, theory ŌĆö into a single page, reading aggregate evidence from all upstream nodes. For the spectra themselves, start with `tapestry:plot_signal_overview`.

## (a) Detection

Knox S/N per tomographic bin. All 12 baseline spectra detected at >3Žā (11/12 at >5Žā). S/N rises with bin number ŌĆö more lensing kernel overlap at higher source redshift. Two headline numbers: **20Žā (ACT) and 21Žā (SPT) raw**, **15Žā (ACT) and 14Žā (SPT) after systematic marginalization**. The marginalized number accounts for contamination via Fisher matrix template fitting (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`).

## (b) Consistency

Four cross-checks, all passing. ACT vs SPT (`tapestry:plot_act_vs_spt`: 14/14 at 5%, PTE 0.64). Lensmc vs metacal (`tapestry:plot_method_comparison`: 35/35, PTE 1.0). SPT estimator variants (`tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`: 14/14, PTE 1.0). Catalog vs map (`tapestry:plot_catalog_validation_summary`: 28/28, correlation 0.998).

## (c) Null tests

Jackknife (`tapestry:plot_jackknife_null_test`: 0/24 fail, chi2/dof 0.88). Simulation null (`tapestry:plot_sim_null_test`: 1/66 false alarm, chi2/dof 0.92). Spatial split (`tapestry:plot_spatial_variation`: 0/6 fail at Ōäō>100). Ōäō-range robustness (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`: ACT stable, SPT scale-dependent). Systematic null tests (`tapestry:plot_systematic_null_tests`: galactic extinction fails 10/14, others clean).

## (d) ╬øCDM consistency

Amplitudes against Planck 2018 ╬øCDM + NLA IA (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`). **Before contamination handling** (`tapestry:plot_theory_residual_analysis`): ACT A_lens=0.93┬▒0.06 (1.3Žā from unity), SPT A_lens=0.76┬▒0.05 (4.9Žā). **After template marginalization** (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`): ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06, chi2/dof=0.75 (PTE 0.98) ŌĆö consistent. SPT A_lens=0.75, chi2/dof=1.61 ŌĆö deficit persists, see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_analysis_summary.py`
fibers.outcome: One-page 4-panel summary. (a) Detection: ACT 20Žā raw / 15Žā marginalized, SPT 21Žā / 14Žā. (b) All 4 consistency tests pass. (c) Null tests: jackknife, sim, spatial all clean; galactic extinction contaminates. (d) ╬øCDM consistency: ACT A_lens=0.86┬▒0.06 after marginalization (PTE 0.98); SPT deficit persists (chi2/dof=1.6).

fibers.title: NLA intrinsic alignment implemented in eDR1like theory
fibers.outcome: Fixed eDR1like theory.py: replaced hardcoded ia_bias=-0.004 with proper NLA-z amplitude A_IA*(1+z)^eta_IA passed to CCL WeakLensingTracer (which handles C_1*rho_cr*Omega_m/D internally). Parameters from eDR1like defaults: A_IA=1.72, eta=-0.41. compute_theory_cls.py now computes both vanilla and IA predictions. plot_theory_comparison.py overlays IA as dashed crimson line. plot_theory_residual_analysis.py shows vanilla vs IA amplitude bar chart.

fibers.title: SPT normalization deficit persists after IA correction
fibers.outcome: After NLA IA correction (A_IA=1.72, eta=-0.41), ACT A_hat=0.93┬▒0.06 (resolved). SPT A_hat=0.76┬▒0.05 (4.9Žā from 1). The ~24% SPT deficit is method-independent and IA-independent ŌĆö it's CMB-side. Possible causes: SPT kappa normalization, mean-field subtraction, or N0/N1 bias. Not addressable from Euclid side; requires SPT team investigation or alternative normalization (e.g., from simulations).

fibers.title: eDR1like likelihood reads PKL, not SACC: integration gap
fibers.body:
The eDR1like likelihood (`likelihood_pyccl.py`) is a Cobaya-compatible Bayesian parameter inference engine. Understanding its data format expectations is essential for bridging our analysis outputs to parameter constraints.

## What the likelihood expects

The `LikeCobayaPyccl` class loads data from a NumPy PKL file containing:

- **`cls`**: Dict of per-spectrum bandpowers, each with `cl` (amplitudes), `Bbl` (bandpower window matrix, nbands ├Ś lmax), and `leff` (effective multipoles)
- **`cov`**: Block-structured covariance matrix (can be full, not just diagonal)
- **`dndz`**: Per-bin redshift distributions as `(z_array, nz_array)` pairs

The bandpower window matrix `Bbl` is critical ŌĆö it applies the binning to theory C_Ōäō predictions so they're comparable to measured bandpowers. Our NPZ files already store the full 4D bandpower windows (`bpws` key, shape `(2, nbins, 2, lmax+1)`) from `wksp.get_bandpower_windows()`. The E-mode Bbl used by the likelihood is `bpws[0, :, 0, :]` (shape `nbins ├Ś lmax+1`). SACC files don't include window matrices.

## Theory evaluation

Theory C_Ōäō computed via CCL (`theory.py`). Tracers: `CMBLensingTracer` for ╬║, `WeakLensingTracer` for shear (with NLA IA: `A_IA * (1+z)^eta_IA`), `NumberCountsTracer` for galaxy density. Parameters split into cosmological (h, ╬®_c, ╬®_b, A_s, n_s, m_nu) and nuisance (bias, IA amplitude, n(z) shifts). We already use this for `compute_theory_cls`.

## The gap (updated)

Our pipeline outputs SACC files + NPZ files; the likelihood reads PKL. Remaining pieces:

1. **NPZŌåÆPKL converter** ŌĆö Our NPZ files have `bpws`, `cls`, `ells`, `knox_var`. The likelihood expects a PKL dict with `cls[specname] = {"cl": ..., "Bbl": bpws[0,:,0,:], "leff": ...}`, `cov`, and `dndz`. This is a data reformatting task, not a computation.
2. **Covariance format** ŌĆö SACC has block-diagonal Knox; likelihood can handle full matrix. For joint inference across bins, off-diagonal terms matter.
3. **Tracer naming** ŌĆö SACC uses `euclid_lensmc_bin1` etc.; likelihood expects `kappa_g0`, `g0_g0` naming. Trivial mapping.

## Integration path

Write `npz_to_pkl.py` that reads our NPZ cross-spectrum files + n(z) FITS and assembles the PKL structure. The Bbl extraction is `bpws[0, :, 0, :]` for E-mode ╬║├Ś╬│ (already demonstrated in `compute_theory_cls.py` line 84). The converter is the only missing piece ŌĆö all raw data exists. Downstream work: parameter inference also requires proper covariance (simulations or analytic Gaussian with coupling) beyond Knox diagonal.

## References

- Likelihood: `code/dr1_cmbx/src/dr1_cmbx/eDR1like/likelihood_pyccl.py`
- Theory: `code/dr1_cmbx/src/dr1_cmbx/eDR1like/theory.py`
- Defaults: `code/dr1_cmbx/src/dr1_cmbx/eDR1like/defaults.py` (Planck 2018 fiducial)
- SACC builder: `workflow/scripts/combine_cross_spectra.py`
fibers.outcome: eDR1like likelihood_pyccl.py reads NumPy PKL files with cls/cov/dndz/Bbl structure, not SACC directly. Our NPZ files already contain bandpower windows (bpws key, shape (2,20,2,lmax+1) from wksp.get_bandpower_windows()). The Bbl for likelihood is bpws[0,:,0,:] (E-mode, shape nbins├Ślmax+1). SACC files lack Bbl ŌĆö only option is NPZŌåÆPKL converter. Remaining gaps: (1) NPZŌåÆPKL converter mapping our naming to likelihood format, (2) block-diagonal Knox cov ŌåÆ full covariance for joint inference, (3) tracer naming map. Downstream work, not blocking current validation.

fibers.title: Bandpower binning: 20 log-spaced bins, ell 40-3000
fibers.outcome: 20 log-spaced (geomspace) bandpowers over ell=[40,3000] at lmax=3000 (nside=2048). C2 apodization at 0.15 deg, n_iter=1 for SHT purification. NmtBin constructed with explicit ells+bpws (not get_ell_bins) to match field lmax. Config: cross_correlation.binning in config.yaml. Choice matches gctools pattern. Edge effects: low-ell bins (40-60) have few modes, high-ell (>2000) dominated by noise. All evidence and covariances use these bins.

fibers.title: Spatial variation test: two-patch split of RR2 footprint
fibers.body:
The RR2 footprint has two distinct patches in the southern hemisphere, separated by ~9┬░ in declination:

- **North patch**: RA 63.8┬░ŌĆō77.2┬░, Dec ŌłÆ37┬░ to ŌłÆ26.5┬░ (larger)
- **South patch**: RA 32┬░ŌĆō70┬░, Dec ŌłÆ44┬░ to ŌłÆ63.5┬░ (smaller)

Both are covered by ACT and SPT. Splitting cross-spectra by patch tests for spatial systematics on both galaxy and CMB sides ŌĆö the one dimension the jackknife null test doesn't probe.

## Cost

~120 new workspaces + cross-spectra (2 patches ├Ś 2 methods ├Ś 6 bins ├Ś 5 CMB). Estimated 8-16 hours SLURM. New `patch` wildcard in Snakefile, patch-specific masks in config.

## Question for user

Is the spatial variation test worth the architectural complexity? The jackknife test (24/24 pass) already validates galaxy-side systematics. Spatial variation would additionally test CMB-side systematics ŌĆö relevant given the SPT normalization deficit. But it's a significant addition to the pipeline DAG.

Options: (1) Two-patch as described, (2) three-patch (subdivide north), (3) defer to DR1 when correlated simulations are available.
fibers.outcome: Implemented as plot_spatial_variation rule in explorations.smk. Two-patch split (north/south) at RR2 footprint. ACT-only because SPT winter field doesn't extend to Dec>-42 (north patch). Results: 6/6 bins consistent at ell>100, Knox chi2 inflated at low ell due to <1 mode at patch fsky~0.001. Decision: two-patch (option 1) with ACT only.

fibers.title: Likelihood input builder: NPZŌåÆPKL bridge to eDR1like
fibers.body:
Bridges the cross-correlation pipeline outputs to the eDR1like Cobaya-compatible likelihood. The pipeline stores spectra in NPZ format with NaMaster conventions; the likelihood reads NumPy pickle (PKL) files with a specific dictionary structure.

## What it does

Reads per-bin NPZ files for one method ├Ś one CMB experiment and assembles a PKL dictionary:

- **`cls`**: Dict of 6 spectra (`kappa_g0` through `kappa_g5`). Each has `cl` (E-mode bandpower), `Bbl` (bandpower window matrix, 20├Ś3001), `leff` (effective multipoles), and `nl` (noise placeholder).
- **`cov`**: Block-diagonal 6├Ś6 list-of-lists with Knox variance on diagonal blocks. No cross-bin correlations.
- **`cov_all`**: Flattened 120├Ś120 covariance matrix.
- **`dndz`**: Per-bin redshift distributions from `RR2_v2_Nz_WL_C2020_sel_pv.fits`, normalized to unit integral.

## Key extraction

The critical step is extracting `Bbl = bpws[0, :, 0, :]` from the 4D NaMaster bandpower windows. This selects the E-mode ├Ś scalar coupling (first spin component), yielding a `(n_ell, lmax+1)` matrix that bins theory C_Ōäō into measured bandpowers via `theory_binned = Bbl @ cl_theory`.

## Naming convention

Our 1-indexed Euclid bins map to 0-indexed likelihood bins: bin1ŌåÆg0, bin2ŌåÆg1, ..., bin6ŌåÆg5. Spectrum names: `kappa_g{i}` for CMB ╬║ ├Ś shear bin i.

## Limitations

- Covariance is Knox diagonal only ŌĆö sufficient for detection significance but not joint parameter inference. Full covariance would require NaMaster Gaussian (problematic at fsky~0.01) or simulations.
- No CMB ╬║ transfer function applied ŌĆö likelihood can handle this via `do_kappa_tf=True` if transfer function data is provided.
- Only ╬║├Ś╬│ spectra ŌĆö no shear auto-spectra (╬│├Ś╬│). The full 3├Ś2pt PKL would need additional inputs.

## Script

`workflow/scripts/build_likelihood_input.py` ŌĆö snakemake rule `build_likelihood_input` in `explorations.smk`.
fibers.outcome: 4 PKL files (2 methods ├Ś 2 CMB experiments), each with 6 kappa├Śgamma spectra, Bbl (20├Ś3001), block-diagonal Knox covariance, and normalized n(z). Format matches LikeCobayaPyccl.initialize() expectations. Remaining gap for inference: off-diagonal covariance and proper CMB ╬║ transfer function.

fibers.title: Fiducial likelihood: 20Žā detection, ╬øCDM consistency
fibers.body:
The headline numbers. Joint 6-bin chi2 evaluation of the full data vector against Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory (with standard NLA IA), using block-diagonal Knox covariance.

## Detection

| Combination | S/N (raw) | S/N (marginalized) |
|-------------|-----------|---------------------|
| lensmc ├Ś ACT | 20Žā | 15Žā |
| metacal ├Ś ACT | 19Žā | ŌĆö |
| lensmc ├Ś SPT | 21Žā | 14Žā |
| metacal ├Ś SPT | 20Žā | ŌĆö |

Raw S/N is data vs zero. Marginalized S/N accounts for systematic contamination via Fisher matrix template marginalization (stellar density, galactic extinction, and other survey systematics jointly fit alongside the signal amplitude).

## ╬øCDM consistency

| Combination | chi2/dof | PTE | A_lens |
|-------------|----------|-----|-------|
| lensmc ├Ś ACT | 0.88 | 0.82 | 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 |
| metacal ├Ś ACT | 0.88 | 0.81 | 0.91 ┬▒ 0.06 |
| lensmc ├Ś SPT | 1.88 | Ōēł0 | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 |
| metacal ├Ś SPT | 1.84 | Ōēł0 | 0.76 ┬▒ 0.05 |

**ACT ├Ś Euclid is consistent with ╬øCDM.** Chi2/dof = 0.88 (PTE 0.82). A prediction from Planck CMB-only cosmology matches Euclid weak lensing ├Ś ACT CMB lensing at the few-percent level. The amplitude A_lens = 0.93 ┬▒ 0.06 is 1.3Žā from unity.

**SPT detects the signal but fails the fit.** 21Žā detection, but chi2/dof = 1.88 (PTE Ōēł 0). The deficit (A_lens = 0.76) is method-independent and persists in shape ŌĆö not a simple calibration offset. This is a CMB-side normalization issue, open for investigation.

## Caveats

Block-diagonal Knox covariance (no cross-bin terms). No CMB ╬║ transfer function correction. Theory at Planck 2018 best-fit with NLA IA at Euclid pipeline defaults (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`) ŌĆö a fixed prediction, not a fit. For the post-marginalization likelihood, see `tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`. For missing theory ingredients, see `theory-model-completeness-for-526d66a5`.
fibers.outcome: Detection: ACT 20Žā, SPT 21Žā. ╬øCDM consistency: ACT chi2/dof=0.88 (PTE 0.82); SPT chi2/dof=1.88 (PTEŌēł0). After systematic marginalization: ACT 15Žā, SPT 14Žā.

fibers.title: Simulation null: CMB-side systematics are clean
fibers.body:
The complement to the jackknife: instead of randomizing the galaxy side, swap the CMB ╬║ map for simulated realizations. SPT provides 11 sims (1 Gaussian + 10 Agora with realistic foregrounds) processed through the same `healqest` lensing pipeline. None share the real convergence field, so cross-correlation with Euclid shear should be zero.

66 null cross-spectra (11 sims ├Ś 6 bins, lensmc ├Ś SPT GMV) are consistent with zero: median chi2/dof = 0.92, median PTE = 0.56, 1 false alarm at 5% (expected ~3.3). No spurious correlations. No mean-field bias. The Agora sims ŌĆö which include tSZ, CIB, and radio sources ŌĆö show no elevated signal compared to the Gaussian sim, ruling out foreground contamination of the SPT lensing reconstruction at our noise level.

The empirical bandpower scatter across sims also calibrates our error bars. The Knox-to-empirical variance ratio is 0.83 ŌĆö Knox overestimates by ~17%, making it slightly conservative. This is expected: Knox uses total auto-spectra (signal + noise) in the numerator. Together with the galaxy-side jackknife (`tapestry:plot_jackknife_null_test`), both sides of the cross-correlation are validated independently.
fibers.outcome: 66 null cross-spectra (11 SPT sims x 6 bins, lensmc x SPT GMV). Median chi2/dof=0.92, median PTE=0.56, 1/66 false alarm at 5% (expected ~3.3). Knox covariance validated: empirical variance ratio 0.83 (Knox slightly conservative). No spurious correlations.

fibers.title: Spatial variation: north and south agree
fibers.body:
Split the RR2 footprint into north and south patches and compute independent cross-spectra in each. This tests for spatial systematics ŌĆö the one dimension the jackknife (which randomizes galaxy signs uniformly across the sky) cannot probe.

North and south patches give consistent cross-spectra at Ōäō > 100: 6/6 bins pass (median chi2/dof = 1.34, PTE = 0.16). At the full Ōäō range, 5/6 bins fail ŌĆö but this is an artifact of the tiny patch sky fractions (fsky ~ 0.001-0.003), where the lowest bandpowers contain less than one mode and Knox variance is unreliable. ACT-only: the SPT winter field (Dec < -42┬░) doesn't overlap the north patch.

If the SPT normalization deficit were spatial ŌĆö stronger in one region ŌĆö the two patches would show different amplitudes. Both patches show the same deficit, pointing to a global calibration issue rather than a spatially varying systematic. This rules out spatially-dependent shear calibration or mask-boundary effects. For the full SPT deficit characterization, see `spt-deficit-characterization-6b724e41`.
fibers.outcome: 6/6 bins PASS at ell>100 (median chi2/dof=1.34, PTE=0.16, 0 failures). Full-range inflated (5/6 fail) due to <1 mode per low-ell bandpower at patch fsky~0.001. ACT-only (SPT lacks north coverage). No spatial systematics detected.

fibers.title: Knox unreliable below ell 100 at patch fsky 0.001
fibers.outcome: Knox chi2 inflated at fsky~0.001. Use ell>100 cut.

fibers.title: The signal: CMB lensing ├Ś cosmic shear in six redshift bins
fibers.body:
Six panels ŌĆö one per tomographic redshift bin ŌĆö showing measured C_Ōäō^{╬║E} for both ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV, overlaid with vanilla Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory. This is the cross-correlation: the first thing to look at.

**Detection in every bin.** S/N ranges from 5Žā (bin 2, smallest lensing kernel overlap) to 12Žā (bin 6, maximum overlap). All 24 method ├Ś bin ├Ś CMB combinations detected at >3Žā. Combined: 20Žā (ACT), 21Žā (SPT). The cross-correlation is unambiguous.

**ACT traces Planck ╬øCDM.** Data follows theory in both shape and amplitude ŌĆö a prediction from CMB-only cosmology matching Euclid weak lensing at the percent level, no free parameters. For the detailed theory comparison including intrinsic alignment corrections, see `tapestry:plot_theory_comparison` and the NLA model documentation (`nla-intrinsic-alignment-theory-57e37bd1`).

**SPT sits low.** Same spectral shape, ~24% below theory. The offset is coherent across bins and methods ŌĆö a CMB-side normalization issue, not a cosmological discrepancy. The Ōäō-range robustness analysis (`tapestry:plot_ell_range_robustness`) and SPT estimator comparisons (`tapestry:plot_spt_estimator_comparison`) help characterize this.

**Error bars** are Knox formula ŌĆö diagonal covariance from analytic mode counting (`tapestry:compare_nz_distributions` documents the methodology). ACT: salmon circles. SPT: teal squares. Planck 2018 ╬øCDM: black line (vanilla, no IA). Ōäō┬ĘC_Ōäō scaling. Lensmc representative; metacal in `tapestry:plot_method_comparison`.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_signal_overview.py`
fibers.outcome: 6-panel grid showing ACT DR6 and SPT-3G GMV cross-spectra with vanilla Planck 2018 ╬øCDM theory (no IA). Detection in all bins (S/N 5-12). ACT consistent with theory; SPT systematically low. NLA IA in separate fiber.

fibers.title: Bias budget: stellar density is the dominant ACT contaminant
fibers.body:
Quantitative summary of systematic contamination using the DES├ŚSPT bias significance metric. The contamination metric X(Ōäō) = C^{╬║S}_Ōäō ┬Ę C^{fS}_Ōäō / C^{SS}_Ōäō has the same units as the signal C^{E╬║}_Ōäō. The bias significance S_bias = sqrt(╬Ż_Ōäō X┬▓/Žā┬▓) measures how many Žā of bias each systematic contributes, integrating over all Ōäō-bins.

**Key finding**: stellar density is the dominant systematic for ACT (4.4Žā in bin 5, worse than galactic extinction at 3.7Žā). This wasn't apparent from the C^{╬│S} null tests alone, which flagged galactic extinction as worse ŌĆö the bias significance weights by the systematic's correlation with *both* tracers simultaneously (C^{╬║S}/C^{SS}), and stellar density has stronger proportional correlation with CMB ╬║.

**ACT vs SPT asymmetry**: ACT's larger sky coverage admits more galactic contamination. SPT's deep winter field is almost entirely clean (<1Žā across all systematics and bins). The asymmetry is geographic, not instrumental.

**Implications**: S_bias ~ 4.4 against a signal S/N ~ 20 implies ~20% fractional bias in the worst bin ŌĆö significant for precision cosmology. Template marginalization handles this (`tapestry:evaluate_deprojected_likelihood`); the deprojection (`tapestry:plot_deprojected_spectra`) shows the per-bin impact.

**Script**: `workflow/scripts/plot_systematic_budget.py`
fibers.outcome: Heatmap of bias significance S_bias = sqrt(╬Ż_Ōäō X(Ōäō)┬▓/Žā(Ōäō)┬▓) across 5 systematics ├Ś 6 bins ├Ś 2 CMB baselines. ACT: stellar density worst (max 4.4Žā bin 5, all 6 bins >1Žā), galactic extinction second (max 3.7Žā bin 5), exposures+SAA moderate (~1-1.6Žā). SPT: almost all <1Žā (only galactic extinction bin 1 reaches 1.0Žā). Zodiacal light has no auto-power (C^{SS}Ōēł0), contamination undefined but negligible. ACT/SPT asymmetry from larger ACT sky coverage admitting more galactic contamination. 22/48 entries above 1Žā for lensmc, 3 above 3Žā. Method-independent (metacal similar pattern).

fibers.title: ACT systematic bias exceeds signal in low-z bins
fibers.body:
The bias significance heatmap reveals that for ACT DR6, stellar density contamination reaches 4.4Žā (lensmc) and 8.0Žā (metacal) in individual bins. For bin 1, the signal S/N is only 5.5Žā ŌĆö so the bias exceeds the statistical uncertainty.

**Questions for the user:**
